name: Barath Arivazhagan
bio: I am a Kubernetes Engineer with over 10 years of experience in cloud and container platforms. I specialize in CNCF technologies including Kubernetes, Helm, GitOps, IaC, and service mesh, with a focus on building scalable, secure, and automated platforms across AWS and Azure.

name: Bayankhangai Khurelchuluun
bio: Just a cusper. Following cloud-native technologies and supporting related systems.

name: Maximilian Braun
bio: Maximilian Braun is an Engineering Manager at SAP where he leads a team of cloud native engineers at the forefront of the digital transformation from traditional enterprise IT to modern cloud-native platforms.He was forged in the human cauldron of DevOps toil (including the genuinely terrifying Friday deployments), emerging with a perspective shaped by Infrastructure-as-Data principles and the DORA metrics ethos. He turned those hard-earned lessons into a philosophy: treat everything as code and data for full reproducibility and auditability, and let key metrics guide continuous improvement. The goal: to banish the fear from deployment days and elevate the developer experience.Today, his work extends to the broader European technology landscape. He is a workstream lead in the European IPCEI-CIS initiative and a NeoNephos Ambassador, advocating for European interoperability and an ecosystem built together on an open-source digital commons. He also champions multiple CNCF and NeoNephos projects and initiatives with his team.Looking ahead, Maximilian is focused on helping build a future where European digital sovereignty is not just a concept but a reality - powered by open, interoperable, and community-driven cloud technologies that empower the next generation of developers.

name: Zhiyuan Gao

I am deeply passionate about the cloud native community and have attended KubeCon events in Shanghai and Hong Kong multiple times. I am highly optimistic about the potential of cloud native technologies in the AI era and am committed to actively contributing to the cloud native community.

Driven by a passion for DevOps and a keen interest in Kubernetes, I am dedicated to fostering innovation in pipeline security and cloud-native technologies to deliver robust, scalable solutions.

I learned so much the last years at the Kubecons and met so many fantastic people. I'm really happy to choosed this way.

Find his projects at github.com/nelssec.

Before that, I held DevOps and SRE roles at VMware in the Security and Cloud Management business units, developing solutions for endpoint security and cloud automation.

I’m deeply passionate about automation, reliability, and the evolving DevOps/SRE ecosystem.

Outside of work, I’m a full-time dad and an avid sports enthusiast, with a love for football, boxing, and MMA. I’m also a board game geek, enjoy poker, and recently discovered a passion for outdoor survival adventures.

She works closely with project maintainers, member companies, and internal stakeholders to increase visibility, manage high-impact announcements, and strengthen CNCF’s global reputation.

name: Paige Cruz
bio: Paige Cruz is passionate about cultivating sustainable on-call practices and believes that open source instrumentation has a bright future. She isn’t afraid to admit that distributed traces are her favorite 😛 and will talk to anyone about adopting OpenTelemetry. After retiring from SRE she brought her zest for bringing people their aha moment with observability to Chronosphere where she works as a Senior Developer Advocate. Read her blog posts over on https://chronosphere.io/learn/category/blog/ or her personal blog at https://www.paigerduty.com/. Off-the-clock you can find her scouring fiber apprenticing at a local sustainable yarn mill or watching trash TV.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Excited to be an ambassador to demystify observability concepts and practices for all levels of technologists. Looking to grow the OpenTelemetry community from end-users to contributors and maintainers while sparking collaboration across company boundaries.

name: Afzal Ansari

Additionally, he has made significant contributions to various CNCF projects, including Meshery, Keptn, and Jaeger. His dedication to the Keptn project earned him recognition as a Keptn Crew member. He also enjoys creating video content and writing blogs to share his experiences in the cloud-native space with like-minded individuals.

name: Frank Murray

With a keen eye for innovation and efficiency, Frank has been involved on several high-impact internal projects, driving significant improvements in deployment speed, reliability, and cost savings through right sizing resources.

I specialize in CNCF technologies, including Prometheus for observability, ArgoCD for declarative delivery, and other tools in the cloud-native ecosystem that enable modern DevOps and SRE practices.  I’m passionate about automation, infrastructure-as-code, and continuous delivery pipelines. My approach is grounded in operational excellence, reliability engineering, and cost-efficient cloud architecture.

I have realized the power of community, in lifting others up and succeeding together, and I love to share it with others. I believe it is my duty in life to share what I've learned in order to make intellectual progress as a human race.

name: Lee Calcote
bio:
Lee Calcote is the Founder of Layer5, where the community helps organizations harness and exploit the value of service meshes. Previously, Calcote stewarded technology strategy and innovation across SolarWinds. He led software-defined data center engineering at Seagate, delivering predictive analytics and modern systems management. Calcote held various leadership positions at Cisco, where he created Cisco’s cloud management platforms and pioneered new, automated, remote management services.
In addition to his role at Layer5, Calcote serves in various industry bodies, including as a SIG chair in Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), and formerly, participated in the delivery of Redfish 1.0 in the Distributed Management Task Foundation (DMTF), and Docker Benchmark 1.0 in Center for Internet Security (CIS). He is a startup advisor, Docker Captain, Cloud Native Ambassador, O’Reilly author, and speaker in the cloud native ecosystem.
Calcote is a maintainer of open source projects, including Meshery, Service Mesh Interface, and Service Mesh Performance. Calcote holds a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a master’s degree in Business Administration from California State University, Fresno and retains a list of industry certifications.
Meetup: Cloud Native Austin

They also contribute to the Organization for Ethical Source.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to be an ambassador because I'm getting to connect with lots of great people across all of the CNCF projects to make the cloud native ecosystem better. In my ambassadorship year, my goals are to take the great speakers we are cultivating in the observability community and help place them in as many general cloud native meetups as possible to spread the word about cloud native observability, to start a streaming series where we work with different CNCF projects to instrument them, and to get the word out about the end user work we've been doing within OpenTelemetry so other projects can better serve end users.

bio: As a Platform Engineer at Infineon Technologies AG, I serve as the technical lead for Cloud Native infrastructure, specializing in Kubernetes, Linux, and Continuous Integration and Delivery. My responsibilities include designing and implementing scalable, resilient platform solutions. These solutions abstract infrastructure complexity, empowering developers to focus on delivering business-critical applications with greater efficiency.

name: Carlos Omar Ravelo Alvarez
bio: With over a decade of experience architecting robust IBM integration solutions, I possess a deep understanding of connecting complex enterprise systems. My expertise spans the full implementation lifecycle, from API design and security to DevOps automation and monitoring. I am now applying these skills to build and support scalable, cloud-native applications within the Kubernetes ecosystem.

name: Erdogan Berke Kocar
bio: Pre-Sales Engineer at Datadog, with expertise as an AWS Cloud Architect specializing in Containers and Serverless technologies. I help Datadog customers adopt DevSecOps best practices by bridging observability, security, and cloud-native architecture. Passionate about Kubernetes, container security, and enabling engineering teams to build reliable, scalable platforms with confidence.

name: Khaled El Ghammarti
bio: Curious IT engineer with 4 years' experience, an open-source advocate and enthusiast for cloud-native technologies. I design, automate and deploy secure infrastructures on Kubernetes and AWS, following a DevSecOps approach and security-by-design. I enjoy tackling the challenges of complex environments using GitLab CI/CD, Ansible, ArgoCD, containerization and IaC. Happy to join the Kubestronaut community.

name: Nassim Sari
bio: I’m working with a Telecom Vendor Mobile Company and have an experience of 20+ years in Telecommunication domain. I enjoy learning Cloud Native, and new technologies. I like swimming, enjoy watching sport like soccer (football), gathering, and having social fun.I'm learning and sharing my experience as lesson learnt, and my hope is to be helpful, to provide insights that can save time and effort, as a contribution, an idea, or problem faced and solved that could be used as an assisting knowledge function for human and AI ML.

name: Julio C. Martinez
bio: I am deeply passionate about Kubernetes and software development, always eager to build scalable, secure, and efficient systems. With a strong focus on CI/CD automation, I take pride in streamlining deployment workflows and accelerating delivery cycles. My technical dedication is matched only by my love for my son—he inspires me daily to grow, lead by example, and pursue excellence in everything I do.

name: Romain Tixier
bio: I am an engineer with a specialization in telecommunications systems, Big Data and IoT infrastructures. I began my career by working on embedded communication routers in the aerospace industry, before moving to the cloud domain as a DevSecOps/SRE engineer on global projects within major telecommunications and space industry companies. Today, I am specialized in automating the deployment and configuration of private cloud platforms, covering the full stack from bare-metal infrastructure to Kubernetes clusters. Passionate about aerospace technologies, I am eager to actively contribute to the adoption and promotion of cloud-native solutions in this field.

name: Wojciech Baranowski
bio: Cloud Solution Architect at Gdańsk University of Technology, working at the intersection of R&D and production-grade infrastructure. I design and build cloud-native systems from the ground up, focusing on Kubernetes-based platforms and complex distributed architectures. My work centers on solving non-trivial engineering problems and turning advanced concepts into reliable, scalable environments.

name: Jules Pichon
bio: With a keen interest in information security and automation, I am eager to discover and learn about the technologies of tomorrow. Perhaps the boundary between the worlds of cybersecurity and artificial intelligence is closer than it seems. Who knows?

name: Marco Maurer
bio: Grüezi from Switzerland!  After drawing schematics and layout PCBs, repairing power supplies and xDSL modems and writing assembler and C for microcontrollers as an electronics technician in 2005, I luckily came to the embedded Linux world: Linux based LAN switches, routers, PBXes.In 2015 I then switched into the public sector to the market leader in public bus transport here in Switzerland for modernizing the IT systems inside the ~2000 busses. We proved that microservices and containers aren't just limited to datacenters and the cloud but are also working great in the edge. Unfortunately there was no K8s for the edge like k3s at this time, so we used docker-compose.  Since 2019 I work as a platform engineer around Kubernetes and its ecosystem.

name: Scott Rigby
bio: Scott is a Brooklyn based interdisciplinary artist and Developer Advocate. He co-founded the Basekamp art and research group in 1998 and the massively collaborative Plausible Artworlds international network. In technology he enjoys helping develop open source software that anyone can use, most recently projects in the cloud native landscape including co-maintaining Helm, Flux, and OpenGitOps. He helps organize groups such as NYC Kubernetes meetup and CNCF GitOps Working Group under TAG App Delivery. In daily decisions large or small, he tries to help make the world a better place for everyone.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I deeply enjoy helping members of the wider open source community, specifically the CNCF and LF project communities. I try to lift others up whenever possible: through co-presenting talks, replying in Slack, amplifying the work of others, welcoming new people to meetings, helping ensure meetings run smoothly, helping with CNCF Working Group and project governance, and generally being an ally. I'm particularly interested in continuing to encourage cross-project collaboration and alignment between people in CNCF TAGs, WGs and projects. I'm also very dedicated to continuing to help new people step up contributor ladders (and encouraging the creation of those ladders when not present), specifically getting involved in things like project pull request reviews, design discussions, and communication processes around projects and WGs.

name: Mathieu Benoit
bio: I'm passionate about Cloud Native Computing technologies driven by Cloud, Security, Kubernetes and Platform Engineering. I'm one of the co-organizers of the CNCF&K8s Meetup in Quebec city (Canada). You will find some of my learnings in my personal blog: https://mathieu-benoit.github.io/ and in my Medium space: https://medium.com/@mabenoit.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a Cloud Native Ambassador will allow me to be closer to the CNCF community, and be connected to more mentors to grow my knowledge and as an individual too. It will also allow me to find more tips to promote content related to CNCF projects, and hopefully I will be able to help and inspire others to be more involved in this community.

name: Tiger Shi
bio: I'm a student at UC Berkeley pursuing a career in software engineering and DevOps. I'm interested in all things cloud native and aspire to be a great engineer like those I have the honor to be featured alongside here one day.

name: António Carvalho
bio: - SRE/Platform Engineer based in Porto/Portugal - Developing and maintaining Kubernetes clusters and other Cloud Native technologies - Focus on GitOps(ArgoCD, Flux), Observability(OpenTelemetry, Grafana Stack)

name: Tsubasa Kajihara
bio: I am an SRE engineer specializing in Kubernetes, Terraform, and Google Cloud Platform. I focus on building reliable and automated cloud infrastructure.

name: Gelu
bio: DevOps Enginnering, OpenSource enthusiast and keen to learn everyday new stuff

name: Evelien Schellekens
bio: Evelien is a Principal Solutions Architect at Elastic. She enjoys sharing knowledge through public speaking and interacting with the technical community. She’s passionate about observability and open source technologies like Kubernetes and OpenTelemetry.

name: Joakim Hval
bio: Armed with a bachelor in Software Engineering and minimal Cloud Native experience, Joakim is driven by his passion for technology and his readiness to face new challenges. This enthusiasm propels him into the Cloud Native and Open Source ecosystems, starting with what he considers one of the most exciting certifications: Kubestronaut.

name: Simon Hess
bio: With a background in electrical engineering, I have extensive experience in communication technologies and embedded systems, where I integrated machine learning techniques into various projects. This interdisciplinary work deepened my interest in AI technologies and modern distributed systems. Recognizing the convergence of these fields within the cloud-native ecosystem, I transitioned into this area to leverage my diverse expertise. In the cloud-native field, I apply my background to develop scalable, efficient, and intelligent solutions that bridge hardware and software domains.

name: Rishabh Singh
bio: I work as a Cloud Engineer with area of expertise being Docker and Kubernetes. I started my Cloud journey in 2016, and quickly developed interest in Docker. In 2019, I began my Kubernetes journey, and since then have been actively using the technology. In my current role, I work as an SME for Azure Kubernetes Service.

name: Tipsukanya Norrasing
bio: Starting my journey as a Chinese language arts student, I am always grateful for the opportunities to learn and grow in the tech industry since my university days. From gradually teaching myself to understand the world of Cloud Native and Kubernetes, I am incredibly happy that these small steps have led me to achieve the Kubestronaut title. I still see myself as a lifelong learner who enjoys exploring DevOps and DevSecOps every day.

name: Antonio Yusta Esplá
bio: I am a Platform Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes and Linux. I approach my work with a mindset of innovation, security, and efficiency, and view continuous learning and improvement as essential keystones to both my work and my life.

name: Florian Judith

When i'm not architecting, coding, or roller blading, i make illustrations to better share my experience on the field (see: https://github.com/fjudith/cloud-native-isometric-icons). I'm focused on platform engineering and actively contributing to the Backstage project and sharing as speaker in Montréal and Québec city CNCF meetups

name: Lavanya Anbalagan
bio: I am an enthusiastic learner with over five years of experience in enterprise container platforms and holds a Kubestronaut title. In addition to my professional achievements, I have a personal interest in exploring new places and maintaining physical fitness.

name: Aniello Serio
bio: Hi all! I’m Aniello, a passionate advocate for Kubernetes, cloud-native technologies, and the open-source ecosystem.  I am deeply committed to promoting the adoption of Kubernetes and CNCF technologies through content sharing, open-source contributions, and active participation in community meetups.  I thrive on connecting with like-minded professionals, mentoring fellow enthusiasts, and engaging with the community.  My expertise lies in architecting and deploying scalable, reliable, and cloud-agnostic infrastructures and applications that adhere to cloud-native principles, as well as building Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs). I strongly leverage GitOps, DevOps best practices, and open-source solutions whenever possible, while maximizing the potential of the cloud.  I am dedicated to driving innovation in cloud-native technologies, contributing to the growth of this vibrant community, and expanding my network with fellow enthusiasts!

name: Ovidiu Valeanu
bio: Principal Engineer with a passion for Kubernetes and cloud-native tech, experienced across AWS, GCP, and on-premises infrastructure. Focused on Platform Engineering and Data & AI on Kubernetes, I enjoy exploring new CNCF projects, using different IaC tools, and designing scalable, secure systems. Always curious, learning, and sharing with the community.

name: Sara Trappetti
bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

name: Lourens Elzinga
bio: Software developer and Platform/DevOps engineer with a focus on building full-stack applications and deploying platforms on Kubernetes and OpenShift using GitOps and CI/CD.

name: Gusty Sapto Ady Prakoso

With experience in the financial services and technology industries, I focus on building resilient and secure infrastructure using CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Terraform, Argo, Falco, Cilium, OPA, Threatmapper, Trivy, OpenTelemetry, LGTM Stack, etc.I actively contribute to internal tech sharing sessions in my company to promote DevSecOps culture and cloud-native best practices.You can find more about me at https://gusty.id.

name: Mohan Kumar Bn
bio: Project Experience: Argo, cert-manager, Cilium, containerd, etcd, Falco, Helm, Istio, k3s, KEDA, Kubernetes, Kubescape, KubeVirt, Kyverno, Longhorn.  Mohan is a seasoned Lead Site Reliability Engineer at Henkel, where he spearheads cutting-edge initiatives to enhance cloud infrastructure and operational efficiency. With deep expertise in Kubernetes, he has designed and implemented solutions for auto-scaling systems, multi-cloud strategies, and managed Kubernetes services. A passionate advocate for open-source technologies, Mohan constantly explores emerging tools, contributes to the Kubernetes ecosystem, and actively participates in community-driven projects.  Beyond his professional work, Mohan is an avid writer who shares his experiences through insightful blogs on DevOps, SRE best practices, and cloud-native innovations, empowering others to excel in the ever-evolving tech landscape. His curiosity for solving complex problems drives him to experiment with new frameworks, collaborate with open-source contributors, and stay at the forefront of industry trends. Mohan’s dedication to simplifying cloud operations and fostering learning within the tech community makes him a sought-after expert and an inspiring thought leader in the field.

name: Matthew Geldert

name: Ryuto Yoshida

Now fully immersed in cloud-native architecture, DevOps, and SRE. Equally comfortable writing code, designing systems, or fixing the thing that "suddenly stopped working". Bringing structure to the madness.

name: Kiran Kumar Reddy
bio: DevOps enthusiast with focus on improving the processes and keeping the applications up and running in the production.

name: Gichul Hong
bio: Started career in the financial/communications field with Java.  Have been working on bigdata and on premise Kubernetes for several years. Love simple and intuitive systems.

name: Kim Sondrup
bio: He is a regular attendee at Cloud Native Aalborg and KCD Denmark and a passionate open-source evangelist. He started his journey as a developer and, later, in 2016, began working with Puppet; with that, he learned and enjoyed what would later be known as GitOps while he also experienced working with lots of Yaml and IaC. After that, he noticed that binary artifact distribution and management were an often overlooked field of expertise. That led him to later greenfield Artifactory for a large enterprise and get involved with everything around it, including its newest biggest user, the company's new Kubernetes clusters. A lot of Kubernetes and Cloud Native later, with some added certifications and today, he is an experienced Cloud Native professional and recognised Kubestronaut.

name: Marcus Noble
bio: Marcus is a platform engineer and his main area of focus in recent years has been around CI/CD, Go, Kubernetes, containers and DevOps but originally started out as a web developer and JavaScript enthusiast. He can be found blogging online at www.MarcusNoble.co.uk and giving talks at meetups & conferences.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to leverage the support of the CNCF to better help others within out community through shared knowledge and experience. I'm excited for the opportunity to meet more amazing people in the cloud native space and learn from their experiences and expertise.

name: Mrinal Kanti Ghosh
bio: I am a Senior DevOps Professional with over 7 years of experience, specialized in Kubernetes, cloud technologies, and automation. I’m passionate about creating solutions that save time and improve operational efficiency.

My expertise lies in linux, AWS Cloud, Redhat Openstack, container orchestration and microservices architecture.

name: İbrahim Temür

Combining hands-on experience with a community-driven mindset to build reliable, scalable, and secure platforms. name: Ahmed Essam Salem

Passionate about scalable and resilient systems, I continuously expand my knowledge in the CNCF ecosystem.

name: Ersin Sari

I help organizations build secure, scalable, and resilient cloud-native systems by combining best practices in automation, platform engineering, and operational excellence. I enjoy turning complex problems into simple, repeatable solutions and sharing real-world lessons learned through in-depth articles on Medium.

I have contributed to the delivery of multiple production-grade applications across different environments.

Kubernetes felt like a natural next step. The Kubernetes ecosystem and the CNCF are very friendly environments and participating at a KubeCon is one of the most inspiring and educational experiences I've had - looking forward to my next possibility to interact with the community.

name: Ahmad Alkurdi
bio: DevOps & Cloud Engineer

name: Ali Ahmed Mengal

His passion for technology has driven his remarkable career in IT, specializing in microservices-based architecture and Cloud Solutions. He has strong experience in designing modular, scalable, and resilient systems. Ali's deep knowledge and proficiency in implementing secure and robust systems ensure the integrity and reliability of his solutions. His commitment to leveraging cutting-edge technologies consistently results in efficient solutions that meet business objectives. Known for navigating complex technical landscapes, Ali is a visionary leader in IT management and delivery.

name: Jeonghoon Han
bio: AI Backend Engineer, DevOps Engineer

That curiosity pulled me into the world of DevOps, where I instantly fell in love with Docker, Kubernetes, and more recently, NixOS. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies, automation, and system design, and I’m always exploring how to make systems more resilient and elegant. When I’m not debugging YAML or optimizing CI pipelines, you’ll probably find me explaining to friends why “just one more container” is a perfectly reasonable hobby

name: Beatrice Forslund

name: Igor Grigorov

I have a strong focus on Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystem technologies, with interests spanning container orchestration, observability, GitOps, and building scalable, resilient platforms. Deeply passionate about DevOps and SRE practices, I continuously enhance my knowledge of modern cloud-native ecosystems. I actively engage with the cloud-native community through knowledge sharing, technical discussions, and community events, and regularly write and speak about cloud-native best practices. Always eager to grow and learn, I am currently exploring AI and ML to integrate these emerging capabilities into future projects.

name: Igor Gouvea

He has a hands-on profile and a holistic view of modern, resilient, and secure environments. He has solid experience with cloud, Kubernetes, CI/CD, IaC, and observability. He supports development teams in standardizing and predicting delivery flows. He is driven by automation, continuous improvement, and innovation. He seeks challenges in SRE and DevOps, as well as sharing knowledge and inspiring the community.

My core expertise lies in architecting secure, scalable systems using technologies like PHP, Laravel, Node.js, and JavaScript, while integrating DevOps best practices and infrastructure automation using AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform. I'm certified across 13 domains, including 7X AWS, 5X Kubernetes, and 1X Terraform, and I focus on delivering resilient, high-performance systems that balance code quality, security, and operational efficiency. In recent years, I’ve expanded into the CNCF ecosystem, working on platform engineering initiatives, CI/CD pipelines, and internal developer tooling that bridge the gap between development and infrastructure. I’m passionate about solving real-world engineering challenges, mentoring teams, and driving continuous improvement across the software delivery lifecycle.

name: Madhu S Mallisetty

As a certified Kubernetes Administrator and a Salesforce certified MuleSoft Integration Architect, Mallisetty possesses a rare combination of skills that places him at the forefront of digital transformation. His architectural designs have resulted in significant improvements, including a 30% increase in operational efficiency for major enterprises, demonstrating his ability to deliver high-impact solutions. Mallisetty's unique ability to seamlessly integrate legacy systems with cutting-edge cloud technologies enables businesses to achieve unprecedented levels of scalability and innovation. His continued contributions, including mentoring and thought leadership, are shaping the future of enterprise integration and cloud-native technologies across industries.

name: Sahil Anand
bio: I am a seasoned Kubernetes specialist and an avid IT and cloud enthusiast. With a wealth of expertise spanning Kubernetes, Cloud, Networking, DevOps, Monitoring, design and architecture, I have established myself as a versatile professional in the tech industry.  My proficiency is not limited to hands-on technical skills; I have also excelled and love to impart knowledge. I have keen interest and have delivered numerous technical trainings on a wide array of topics such as Cloud, Kubernetes, DevOps, Networking, and Troubleshooting, etc. helping many professionals and organizations advance their technological capabilities.

name: Emmanuel Torrado
bio: I'm a regular guy who just loves tech, crypto, series and spend time with family and friends, i enjoy work as much as hanging out with them, father of 2 cats and a dog!

name: Cailyn Edwards
bio: Cailyn is a Senior Infrastructure Security Engineer, with a passion for Kubernetes and the open source world. She is an active contributor to Kubernetes SIG-Security, SIG-CLI and a 2022 Kubernetes Contributor Award recipient. Her current focus is on network, and Kubernetes multi-tenancy security and she gets to spend her working hours contributing up stream. It brings her great joy to spend time with the CNCF community and empower folks to become involved. Outside of work Cailyn can be found running, playing squash, walking her dogs or working in the garden.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am *so* excited to be a CNCF ambassador because this community has been incredibly impactful to my growth in the industry and I want to give back. I cannot wait to help share all the wonderful things happening at CNCF and organize events for members new and old. One of my favourite parts of my job is mentoring, and I look forward to being able to do this in the Cloud Native community! I want to ensure that each new member has the same welcoming and positive experience that I did!.

name: Shedrack Akintayo

I am currently a Technical Marketing Engineer, Isovalent at Cisco responsible for running the Cilium and eBPF communities. I’m skilled in DevOps with Go, Linux, Shell Scripting, Terraform, Cloud Native Technologies (Kubernetes, Cilium)

name: Yao-Siang Su

In addition to cloud-native technologies, he also enjoys reading and learning the fundamentals of distributed systems and finds great joy in it.

name: Unmesh Shailendra Kadam

I really like working with people that have different skills so we can help each other in improving ourselves and getting the best results. name: Everton Andrade
bio: Solutions Architect with deep expertise in Kubernetes, AWS, and DevSecOps practices. Passionate about cloud-native technologies, infrastructure as code, automation, and secure architecture design.

My present focus is on embedding cloud-native technologies early in the design process to build scalable, secure, and highly available deployments.

Outside of work a curious learner, and someone who believes that great architecture is as much about people as it is about technology.

Meet-Up Organizer:
New York Kubernetes Meetup

name: Ali Ok

He has a strong track record of contributions to various projects, communities, and foundations, and he is currently an active contributor to the Knative project. In addition to his technical contributions, Ali is also deeply committed to mentoring and promoting contributor growth within the CNCF community. He plays an active role in the Google Summer of Code program at CNCF, where he serves as an admin. He is also a frequent speaker on topics such as starting to contribute to open-source software and the projects he is working on.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF Ambassador is an exciting opportunity for me to expand my reach and impact within the community. As someone who is already involved in mentoring and contributor growth activities at CNCF, I'm eager to use the ambassador title to promote CNCF technologies, open-source software, and help interested individuals get started with contributing to CNCF projects. Additionally, being part of a network of other CNCF Ambassadors will allow me to collaborate and share ideas with like-minded individuals who are passionate about the same things. I'm excited to be part of this community and look forward to making a meaningful impact.

Passionate about learning, I’ve built a large-scale home lab with multi-cluster Kubernetes setups and CNCF projects to deepen my understanding of cloud-native solutions. I enjoy sharing knowledge through mentoring, technical discussions, and contributing to the growth of sysadmin, DevOps, and platform engineering teams.

name: Tijmen Van Den Brink
bio: As a Lead Solution Architect I design cloud native platforms that enable organizations to run critical workloads securely and reliably. Over the past five years, I played a key role in developing a private cloud platform for the dutch government—from winning the tender through design, implementation, and operations. Building on this foundation, I'm now driving our Cloud Native portfolio, including Kubernetes as a Service and S3 Object Storage (AIstor/MinIO). My current focus is scaling these offerings across our client base. Together with my international team, we're strengthening clients' security posture through expanded services: policy enforcement with Kyverno, container runtime protection, CVE scanning, and ArgoCD for GitOps delivery. With an MSc in System and Network Engineering from the University of Amsterdam, I specialize in platform engineering and transforming successful client-specific solutions into scalable product offerings. My approach combines hands-on technical expertise with portfolio-level thinking—ensuring individual solutions benefit the wider organization.Technical focus areas: Platform as a Product (Kubernetes as a Service, Object Storage), Container security and policy enforcement (Kyverno), GitOps and CI/CD (ArgoCD, Flux) Observability (Grafana, Prometheus). What drives me is bridging the gap between client-specific innovation and reusable platform services—taking what works at scale for one organization and evolving it into offerings that deliver value across multiple contexts.

I live where DevOps and security crash into each other and call it “collaboration.”  By day, I help teams at scale ship faster and safer — cutting through red tape, automating the nonsense, and teaching others how to build cloud-native systems that don’t crumble under compliance. I’ve spent years in the trenches of Kubernetes, container hardening, CI/CD pipelines, and all the alphabet soup that makes enterprise engineers sweat.  I’ve got a PhD in Information Assurance, but don’t worry — I use it for good. I write policies that make sense, tools that make life easier, and training that helps people actually get it.  When I’m not wrangling YAML or chasing zero CVEs, I’m running King Butter — my hot sauce and seasoning brand that’s fit for royalty — and recording music under my label, Royal Anthem Records. Because life’s too short not to mix a little flavor into your build.

I specialize in Kubernetes orchestration, container security, CI/CD pipelines, and service mesh technologies across the CNCF landscape. A passionate community builder, I organize local cloud-native meetups and mentor aspiring engineers, committed to elevating Pakistan's presence in the global tech ecosystem.

name: Jagoudel Denis
bio: Automation is my credo. A convinced Pythonist. A lover of hacking. And also a big user of Kubernetes, whether onpremise or via cloud providers.

name: Semjon Nordmann
bio: Platform Engineer with expertise in building secure, scalable cloud-native solutions. Passionate about creating developer-friendly tooling that maintains security and compliance standards in containerized applications. Experienced in implementing GitOps workflows, infrastructure-as-code, service mesh architectures, and multi-cluster management strategies.

name: Valentin Hristev
bio: I'm a Cloud Native consultant and trainer with over two decades of experience. I specialize in keeping cloud environments clear and storm-free, helping companies implement cutting-edge solutions. Passionate about innovation and embracing challenges, I dedicate myself to empowering individuals through continuous learning. I also actively participate in non-profit organizations and community events. Outside of work, I enjoy spending time with my dog and exploring my passion for cars. My dedication to technology and personal growth is complemented by my drive to master life's bumpy and uncertain courses.

name: Johannes Wetzel
bio: Reality is fluid and malleable.  DevOps Engineer - Kubernetes, Linux, Strimzi Kafka, Cilium, GitOps ..multi-intrested, always learning, with a strong passion for complexity, mutual support and cooperation

name: Stefan Möhrle
bio: Stefan is the co-founder and DevOps consultant at corewire GmbH, specializing in Kubernetes, AWS, and CI/CD pipelines. With a strong focus on DevOps, DevSecOps, and developer experience, he helps teams optimize their workflows through hands-on training and consulting. Stefan is also an active member of the DevOps community, maintainer of the keycloak-provider for crossplane and takes part in organizing the DevOps Meetup Freiburg.
location: Freiburg im Breisgau, Baden Württemberg, Germany

name: Yashkumar Barot
bio: I am a skilled Site Reliability Engineer with over 4 years of expertise in cloud technologies, containerization, and Infrastructure as Code (IaC). I excel in automating cloud infrastructure, optimizing deployment processes, and have a specialty in multi-cloud environments. My experience also includes working as a DevOps Engineer, enhancing my ability to build and maintain resilient systems. Passionate about CNCF technologies, I hold numerous certifications. In my free time, I enjoy playing cricket, video games, and reading books.

name: Kristoffer Eikså
bio: Kristoffer is an infrastructure engineer with a background in cybernetics and robotics. He is currently enjoying learning about everything the world of CNCF has to offer.

name: Richard Hartmann
bio: Richard "RichiH" Hartmann is the Director of Community at Grafana Labs, Prometheus team member, OpenMetrics founder, OpenTelemetry member, CNCF TAG Observability chair, CNCF Governing Board member, and more. He also leads or organizes various conferences, including PromCon, FOSDEM, DENOG, DebConf, and Chaos Communication Congress. In the past, he made mainframe databases work, ISP backbones run, freenode work, and designed and built a datacenter from scratch. Go through his talks at https://github.com/RichiH/talks or follow him on Twitter at https://twitter.com/TwitchiH for musings on the intersection of technology and society.

name: Arthur Tira
bio: I'm  a software engineer with a keen interest in Cloud-native technologies. Always eager to learn, constantly seeking ways to expand my knowledge and stay at the forefront of technological advancements.

name: Jose Javier Acosta Santana
bio: Hi, I'm Jose! I love boosting productivity and automating processes. With a focus on Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, and scalable cloud infrastructure, I excel at driving efficiency at every stage of the software development lifecycle. Remember, learning never stops! 🚀
location: San Cristóbal de la Laguna, Spain

name: Mario Constantin
bio: From a university student with zero cloud-native knowledge just three years ago to a Cloud Engineer and Kubestronaut, my journey through the CNCF ecosystem has been both challenging and rewarding. I’m hands-on, detail-oriented, and a problem-solver, but above all a team player. My experience spans AWS, GCP, and Azure, incorporating CNCF projects like Kubernetes, ArgoCD, Istio, Cilium, and Prometheus. I enjoy sharing knowledge, mentoring peers, and engaging with community events, and I’m currently building marioconstantin.dev to inspire collaboration in cloud and Kubernetes spaces.

name: Lkhagvatuguldur Demberel
bio: Hello from 🇲🇳 Mongolia! I'm a Software Engineer and DevOps enthusiast who loves building reliable systems and solving tough technical problems. Passionate about cloud-native technologies and continuous learning.

name: Brian Shand
bio: I am a DevOps Engineer specializing in building and operating Kubernetes platforms in AWS, with a strong focus on GitOps workflows. I work extensively with EKS, container orchestration patterns, and automated Cl/CD pipelines to help ship reliable cloud-native applications. I stay active in the CNCF community and regularly attend conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon.
location: North Palm Beach, Florida, United States of America

name: Elyes Jarroudi
bio: am Elyes Jarroudi, a DevOps Engineer with a strong focus on designing and managing scalable, secure infrastructure on AWS and Kubernetes. I specialize in cloud migrations, cost optimization, and implementing monitoring and security best practices to ensure resilient systems. I enjoy sharing my expertise through technical blogs and engaging with the cloud-native community to drive innovation.

name: Alicia Dunn
bio: Senior Cloud Architect | Multi-Cloud | KubernetesI'm a Senior Cloud Architect with a deep passion for continuous learning and evolving technologies. With a strong foundation built over years in Network Engineering, Application Security, Platform Engineering, DevOps, and Automation, I bring a holistic approach to cloud-native architecture and delivery.  My professional journey includes working as a Senior Application Security Engineer in corporate environments, where I developed a security-first mindset that now informs my cloud and platform strategies. Recently, my focus has shifted to mastering and integrating multi-cloud platforms—particularly AWS, Azure, and Oracle Cloud.  I thrive in the dynamic and ever-evolving Kubernetes landscape and find great satisfaction in designing scalable, resilient cloud-native systems. I'm an active supporter and learner within the CNCF community and completely grateful for the experience, thus far.

name: Braeden Earp
bio: Braeden Earp builds cloud-native platforms with a focus on Kubernetes, service mesh, and infrastructure automation. He enjoys solving complex problems at scale and has led teams supporting secure, high-performance workloads—including ML and GPU-based systems. He’s passionate about open source, clean architecture, and making platforms developers actually enjoy using.
location: Willow Springs, North Carolina, United States

name: Igor Beliakov
bio: Ex-network engineer (switched my focus to Kubernetes in 2019), one of maintainers of grafana-operator (https://github.com/grafana/grafana-operator/)

name: Praveen Chigurupati
bio: I’m a Solution Architect passionate about blending Generative AI, cloud-native platforms, and data systems into something smarter (and occasionally sassier). I design AI agents that thrive on Kubernetes and build operators that can practically manage themselves. Lately, I’ve been exploring eBPF to peek under the hood of the kernel, because observability is cooler when it feels like magic. I’m focused on bringing AI and cloud-native technologies closer together to create platforms that are smarter, adaptive, and built for the future.

name: Rahul Rai
bio: I am a results-oriented Systems Engineer and automation advocate, skilled on Linux, Virtualization, and Automation. My core expertise lies in Infrastructure tools and orchestration using GitHub Actions, Ansible, Packer, Puppet, Golang, Python etc.  In free time, I love to cook and spend time with my little one. I am eager to transition to microservices deployment and orchestration as well. Excited and looking forward to it.

name: Chandrashekar B
bio: I’m a passionate Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) specialising in cloud-native and open-source technologies. I have expertise in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable cloud infrastructures, with a focus on reliability, automation, and cost efficiency. I enjoy driving innovative and efficient solutions that enhance performance and empower engineering teams.

name: Miley Fu
bio: Miley has extensive experience in DevRel and has worked on the WasmEdge project for over 3 years from its very start. She implements strategies to nurture developer communities, explores content marketing, and engages with devs on the WasmEdge repo to maintain an active/ responsive presence on GitHub. She hosts tech meetups and conferences online and in-person, including OpenSource Fairs, WebAssembly Meetups and Rust language meetups and conferences. Check out the videos of WasmEdge's most recent meetups in Silicon Valley and Singapore: https://github.com/second-state/meetupsWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to be a CNCF ambassador because I am passionate about running cloud-native technologies workshops and communities and contributing to the ecosystem's growth. Working closely with a supportive community enables me to learn from the experiences of others and share valuable insights. Moreover, being an ambassador provides an excellent platform for WasmEdge to engage with other projects and stay up-to-date on the latest cloud-native advancements. I can not wait to connect with other ambassadors

name: Roberto Tagliabue
bio: I'm a Senior Cloud Solution Architect specializing in AWS technologies. I work extensively with EKS clusters and am familiar with many CNCF projects. My favorites are Argo CD, Kyverno, and Karpenter. I'm deeply interested in Kubernetes world and continuous learning and enjoy exploring innovative solutions to improve cloud architectures and automation.

name: Andrei Rusnac
bio: Coming from the Networking and Linux field I am a DevOps engineer who loves spending time discovering new things and troubleshooting infrastructures. I like to research new methods of optimizing products and to find extravagant ways to solve the unsolvable.

name: Ben De Haan
bio: I am a Security/DevOps Consultant, Trainer and Engineer, and co-project leader of OWASP WrongSecrets. My specialties are architecting and implementing cloud native services and building secure CI/CD environments. I enjoy public speaking and hosting trainings or workshops, and I’m an OWASP NL Meetup regular.

name: Justin Watulingas
bio: I'm a Platform Engineer and Kubernetes enjoyer focused on building robust Cloud Native platforms that balance performance, automation, and security. I enjoy simplifying complex systems and creating reliable foundations that help engineering teams deliver with confidence. Staying current with emerging tools, security practices, and platform patterns is a key part of my approach. I actively pursue opportunities to deepen my expertise through continuous learning and aim to create solutions that are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with long-term organizational needs.

name: Regina Voloshin
bio: I am an OSS Tech Lead at Codefresh by Octopus Deploy, an ArgoCD maintainer and involved with the Argo community. I am also a speaker at CNCF events (GitOpsCon, ArgoCon, KubeDay Israel). I also actively encourage and inspire others to contribute to CNCF projects and to speak at conferences. Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I love being part of and collaborating with the cloud-native OSS community. In addition to the great benefit of using OSS, I strongly believe that adopting the methodology used to write, maintain and release OSS widely benefits any organization. CNCF makes cloud-native OSS accessible to everyone, and I am excited to take part in fostering the CNCF ecosystem and increasing the OSS adoption.

name: Michael Hajjar
bio: I am a freelance Linux, Kubernetes and Security engineer. Owner of CloudyLinux. Love working on complex problems to create and combine innovative solutions in the CNCF-ecosystem.

name: Oussama Algouti
bio: I'm a Senior DevOps & Cloud Consultant focused on automating cloud infrastructure on AWS using Terraform, Kubernetes (EKS), and cloud-native tools from the CNCF ecosystem such as Argo CD, Prometheus, Loki, and Helm. I specialize in GitOps workflows, cost optimization (FinOps), and secure CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions. I help engineering teams migrate to the cloud and adopt DevOps best practices.

name: Andre Bojiko
bio: Holder of a Research Master's degree in Computer Science, I have worked as a Cloud & DevOps Engineer as well as Solution Architect and Consultant in Bank and Environment companies.  I am focused on Kubernetes security, policy enforcement, and runtime hardening. I advise teams and companies on how to build secure, cloud migration, scalable platforms, from pipelines to production clusters in both Cloud and OnPremise environments.  Sharing my knowledges, operational excellence, customer obsession and continuous learning are my Passion.

name: Tristan Smagghe
bio: I'm a software engineer at Infomaniak and mostly work on Kubernetes. I also present talks from time to time and the first one I presented was at Silicon Chalet in Geneva. You can find out more about me at https://yewolf.fr

name: Justin Lex-Hammarskjöld
bio: Justin is a passionate Cloud/On-Prem Architect with in-depth experience in cloud architecture, cybersecurity, DevOps, and software development. He works as a DevOps consultant based in Stockholm, with a specialization in Kubernetes and other Cloud-Native technologies. Justin is also involved in helping to organize the Cloud-Native Community meetups in Stockholm, and occasionally gives talks. He enjoys the rapid pace that the Cloud-Native community has been building new technologies for the future, and is excited every year to see where Cloud-Native will go next.

name: Roberto Gomez
bio: I am a Solutions Architect specializing in IT automation with VMware Aria Automation (now VCF Automation) and VMware Tanzu. Coming from the world of virtualization, and making my way as a DevOps / SRE.

name: Dibyajyoti Hazra
bio: Dibyajyoti Hazra is a Senior Backend Developer with over 8 years of experience in building scalable distributed systems. His expertise spans networking (DHCP/IPAM, DNS), cloud and IaC security, plugin and extension development, and LLM-based backend applications. He is proficient in GoLang and has hands-on experience with containerization and orchestration technologies like Docker and Kubernetes, as well as cloud platforms such as AWS. Link to blog: https://dibyahazra.github.io/

name: Ahmed Doban
bio: I'm a Cloud Technical Lead with extensive experience designing and managing cloud solutions across AWS, GCP, and Oracle Cloud. I specialize in cloud architecture, infrastructure automation, and platform engineering, helping teams build scalable and resilient systems.

name: Josh Gavant
bio: Josh is passionate about enabling project builders and end users to succeed with cloud computing. He's a solution architect for Red Hat and leader of CNCF's TAG App Delivery and WG Platforms. He also leads the CNCF Chicago community group and other Chicago-area groups. Check out Josh's blog at https://blog.joshgav.com/!Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to continue enabling beginners and experts alike to make the most of cloud computing! As an ambassador I'll be even more empowered to educate and support Chicago-area developers in our local communities. I'm looking forward to sharing knowledge myself, but most of all I'm looking forward to enabling people in my communities to share their knowledge with each other!

name: Mohmmad Rouf
bio: I work as a Kubernetes Infrastructure Engineer at VoerEir, specializing in deploying and managing cloud-native environments for high-performance applications. My expertise includes cloud-native infrastructure deployment, CNF resource management, and performance optimization, particularly for time-sensitive systems like Open RAN, Edge Computing, and AI-driven workloads. I am passionate about Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies, focusing on integration, scalability, and optimization, while staying engaged with emerging trends and best practices.

name: Davanum Srinivas
bio: Davanum Srinivas (a.k.a Dims) is a Principal Engineer with Nvidia working on Kubernetes, containerd and related projects at CNCF. Dims is involved in various Kubernetes SIGs and working groups like the Architecture, Testing, Node, Infrastructure etc.

name: Shijun Hou
bio: I am currently mainly engaged in work related to the technical architecture of cloud computing products. I have worked in IT companies such as Tencent and NetEase, and have about 20 years of experience in R&D, architecture design, and operation and maintenance. I always focus on areas such as cloud native, distributed middleware, and IT architecture transformation. I am a contributor to open source software such as Kube-router, Redis-operator, Polaris, and a speaker in the cloud native community. I have participated in the formulation of more than ten cloud computing industry standards and have more than twenty technical invention patents in the field of cloud computing.

name: Huan Wei
bio: Huan has more than 15 years of work experience, and his current focus is cloud-native edge computing, distributed cloud, and high-performance computing. He is currently working as a Senior Technical Director at HarmonyCloud, a company located in Hangzhou, China, providing leading cloud-native products and solutions for hundreds of enterprise customers, including Kubernetes, PaaS, edge computing, micro service visibility, DevOps, etc. In addition, Huan serves as a Technical Steering Committe member of the CNCF edge computing project KubeEdge. Huan is an open source technology enthusiast and is currently committed to building an open source technology ecosystem in China.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The company I work for, HarmonyCloud, is an influential cloud native tehnology supplier in China and a long-term partner of CNCF. HarmonyCloud has helped many customers around the world complete the transformation of cloud-native technology. At the same time, I am currently working as a TSC member in the CNCF Project KubeEdge community, responsible for leading several SIG teams and working with other TSC members towards the graduation of the KubeEdge project. Over the years, I have also given speeches at conferences organized by CNCF. Therefore, I am familiar with the operation process of CNCF projects and understand the job responsibilities of CNCF ambassadors. If I become an ambassador, I will promote the maturity and promotion of Kubernetes, KubeEdge and more open source projects, and help users around the world to apply these advanced and open source cloud-native tenologies.

name: Marco Van Kimmenaede
bio: I'm Marco van Kimmenaede living with my wife and daughter in Hippolytushoef. Almost 25 years experience in IT infrastructure/Linux and last year’s mostly my focus on Kubernetes and the CloudNative landscape.

name: David Watts
bio: Hi there! I'm Dave, and I'm deeply passionate about all things CNCF, especially Kubernetes. I believe true leadership isn't about rank or authority but about the choice to look after the person to the left of you and the person to the right of you. My approach to DevOps is rooted in breaking down silos, emphasizing the importance of people skills and communication to foster trust and collaboration within teams. Ensuring that everyone feels supported and empowered to succeed is what drives me.

name: Remco Stolwijk
bio: I'm a cloud-native engineer from the Netherlands. Since the start of my career, I've been working with Kubernetes and a wide range of CNCF projects. I love building scalable, modern platforms and exploring new open-source tech. You might spot me at KubeCon.

name: Johannes Schnatterer
bio: Grown up as a developer, by now I have spent more time being a cloud engineer/platform engineer, bringing me to my present role as Field CTO of Cloudogu. Along the way, I contributed to two books (GitOps+K8s security), gave several dozen talks, wrote close to 80 articles, and trained more than 100 people in Containers, Kubernetes, and GitOps. In addition, I created open source projects like gitops-playground (GOP) and community resources like gitops-patterns, k8s-diagrams, k8s-security-demos, and most recently ckad-cka-cks-kubestronaut.  I first deployed on K8s 1.7 🙂

name: Kimihiko Abe
bio: I am conducting research and development on data processing infrastructure leveraging Kubernetes and accelerators such as GPUs and FPGAs.Additionally, I am working on Layer 3 network verification technologies utilizing container-based routing technologies.

name: Julia Okuniewska
bio: Cloud Software Engineer focused on Golang and Kubernetes, moving from on-prem into public cloud providers. My background includes load testing with k6, scaling KubeVirt, observability (Prometheus+Grafana, now Dynatrace), CI/CD pipelines with GH Actions/Jenkins + ArgoCD and contributions to opensource - Cluster API and Telegraf projects.

name: Tobias Lahrmann Hansen
bio: Tobias is a seasoned Linux and Cloud Native enthusiast with hands-on experience running Kubernetes clusters on-premise and in the cloud. His practical knowledge and adaptability in these environments make him a valuable asset to any tech team.  While enjoying his advanced Linux computer janitor role, he slowly got tired of the toil involved with hand-rolling new servers and juggling shared libraries. Seeking a better way to bin-pack applications, he began advocating the use of containers; however, this didn't remove the need to hand-roll servers, so his journey led him to explore Kubernetes, where he quickly gained a taste for monitoring workloads with Prometheus and empowering his colleagues to make metrics-driven decisions.  Having worked with Kubernetes and its abstractions, Tobias still prefers his simple command line for most tasks. He firmly believes in the UNIX philosophy and KISS principles, even if daily driving Emacs might go against these principles.

name: Zakariae El Alaoui El Belghiti
bio: Passionate about development, I have started my journey as a PHP developper then tech lead .net to occupy now a position of Cloud Technical Architect. Continuous learning is my mojo and this is how I came to learn and  explore Kubernetes. In my non virtual life, I enjoy runing and hiking.

name: Dinesh Gopathy Padmanabhan
bio: Seasoned Professional Services Consultant & Instructor, with expertise in Infra, Automation, Cloud and Cloud Native technologies. Highly experienced in Architecting, Designing and Implementing Virtualization, Cloud Management and Automation solutions for several major customers across ASEAN, Middle East and India. A former VMware Certified Instructor who has trained over 1000 students. Holds over 50 certifications in Virtualization, Cloud, Cloud Native and DevOps technology domains.

name: Elena Neroslavskaya
bio: Elena is a Cloud Solution Architect who’s passionate about AI, Kubernetes (especially CNCF), and all things open source. She loves exploring new tech, sharing ideas with the community, and building cool stuff in the cloud. She’s also a proud KubeCon speaker—always up for a good challenge and a great conversation.

name: Kyle Hsu
bio: I am Kyle from Taiwan, with over 15 years of hands-on experience in systems and cloud technologies. Throughout my career, I have held roles including Linux System Engineer, Senior System Engineer, Project Lead, and Technical Manager. My expertise covers Linux/Windows system administration, hybrid cloud architecture design, storage and server integration, and enterprise-grade high availability solutions.  Although I started my career as an infrastructure engineer, I have developed a strong interest in Kubernetes. I am grateful for the CNCF Kubestronaut Program, which gave me deeper insights into cloud-native environments. I believe that certifications are only the beginning — continuous learning is the true goal. I look forward to engaging with experts from around the world to exchange knowledge, broaden my perspective, and further sharpen my skills.

name: Nancy Chauhan
bio: I like hacking through software engineering problems. Over the past 3-4 years, I have been developing solutions for software reliability. I love everything around cloud-native, open source, and security. I have also founded Women in Cloud Native community for women who are interested in learning more about Cloud Native and those who are willing to learn, collaborate and support each other to grow Website - https://womenincloudnative.com/ Personal Blog - https://nancychauhan.in/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I look forward to contributing to the CNCF community as an ambassador and keep doing things I was doing before. Meeting other like-minded ambassadors and motivating each other to contribute, support, and inspire others excites me

name: Yuya Yamanaka
bio: As an IT Infrastructure Engineer at Nomura Research Institute (NRI), I specialize in Kubernetes, containers, Linux, IaC (including Ansible and Terraform), cloud platforms (OCI, Google Cloud, and on-premises cloud), and internet security.  Our team builds and maintains several platforms that host a wide variety of applications for numerous securities companies. Since these platforms are internet-facing, I have experience in investigating internet security and designing WAF policies.  By building Kubernetes-based container infrastructure in our on-premises environment as well as in dedicated regions of OCI, we contribute to the provision of secure and reliable systems for our clients.

name: Arthur Fewell
bio: I am a Technical Marketing Engineer for Hedgehog, a Kubernetes-based, Open Source software-defined networking platform for the CNCF Sonic Network OS. I have been teaching Kubernetes courses for almost a decade now.

name: Jeffrey Sica
bio:
Jeffrey (@jeefy) is Head of Projects at the CNCF, with a focus on improving community and project automation. Before that, he's worked at Red Hat and the University of Michigan focusing on Cloud Native technologies and CICD patterns. Jeffrey has been a contributor to upstream Kubernetes, helping in SIG-Contribex, SIG-Release, and SIG-UI. He passionately advocates for open source development and recognizing and alleviating burnout.

Meetups: Board Member for Orchestructure

name: Soojin Lee
bio: Hello. I'm Soojin Lee, a Cloud Architect and GKE Engineer at Megazone Soft.  Through my experience as a Google Workspace engineer, I have been providing support and technical advice on governance (such as Cloud ID), which has led me to become interested in Keycloak and related cloud-native systems.  I enjoy sharing my knowledge and experiences with Kubernetes through my blog and by actively participating in the community.  I'm also one of tech ambassadors for Naver Cloud and co-host of Pangyo user meetups in the Naver Cloud User Community.  - My blog: lifeoncloud.kr  - Naver Cloud User Community: https://www.meetup.com/navercloud-user-community/

name: Yusuke Tanaka
bio: I am a systems architect with extensive experience in designing and building large-scale infrastructure for clients in the telecommunications industry.I focus on building scalable and reliable platforms that can adapt to rapidly changing business requirements and technological trends. I have practical expertise in Linux systems, storage, and cloud-native technologies centered around Kubernetes (DevOps, observability, chaos engineering, container security, etc.). Currently, I am also involved in the design and operation of AI/ML platforms.

name: Robert Volkmann
bio: As an experienced and passionate Cloud Native Architect, I accelerate my clients' Private Cloud journeys by designing and implementing customized, high-performance, and secure on-premise bare-metal Kubernetes platforms. I leverage my technical expertise in CNCF technologies to guide cross-functional teams in orchestrating implementation strategies, optimizing system performance, and driving scalable cloud-native solutions. Additionally, I promote the long-term success and sustainability of the infrastructure by providing tailored training for employees.

name: Yong Gu
bio: As an experienced cloud architect, I specialize in transforming visionary concepts into scalable, high-impact solutions that drive enterprise success. With a proven track record of evolving proof-of-concept initiatives into fully optimized, production-ready systems, I excel at addressing complex architectural challenges with precision and innovation. My work is driven by a passion for emerging technologies and a commitment to continuous learning, enabling me to deliver cutting-edge, future-proof solutions in dynamic and rapidly evolving cloud ecosystems.

name: Lucian Cimpeanu
bio: As a Cloud and Platform Engineering Lead, my expertise lies in designing and implementing sophisticated cloud solutions that align with customers' business and technical requirements. My role involves guiding teams through complex cloud migrations, modernizing applications for cloud environments, and conducting comprehensive well-architected reviews.

name: Ihor Matsapura
bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer specializing in cloud computing and automation. I’m passionate about AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform. Holding a master's degree in Computer Engineering, I always seek opportunities to learn something new.

name: Amol Gaikwad
bio: I am a passionate technologist and cricket enthusiast (the sport, not the insect!), always learning and staying up to date with new technologies. I am flexible and dynamic, ready to adapt to new challenges, and have worked in top companies in the oil and gas and fintech industries.

name: Gaurank Goyal
bio: I am a Cloud Architect at Siemens Energy with over 10 years of experience designing solutions on cloud and multi-cloud environments. I am passionate about continuous learning and have expertise in AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, Terraform, and Python.

name: Igor De Beijer
bio: Software Engineer and a builder at heart with a passion for platform engineering. I build with and on top of Kubernetes, focusing on platforms, operators, and extending the K8s API. I maintain a little tool to juggle k8s contexts: https://github.com/idebeijer/kubert. Occasionally, I like to write at https://debeijer.io/blog.
location: Millingen aan de Rijn, Gelderland, Netherlands

name: Uroš Žugić
bio: Helping businesses automate the deployment of their applications as well as using other Dev and Ops tools. Always up for a laugh.

name: Lorenzo Bassi
bio: I'm an Integration Platform Engineer with a strong interest in the cloud-native ecosystem, including Docker, Kubernetes, and related technologies. I enjoy working on automation, infrastructure integration, and building scalable, containerized environments. I'm passionate about staying up to date with modern DevOps practices and CNCF tools.

name: Joshua Taylor
bio: Experienced Devops Engineer working with Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, and various cloud deployments throughout AWS, GCP, & Azure. I enjoy working on projects in Go and Rust. Outside of work, I love learning C++ and C# for video game development in Unreal Engine and Unity.

name: Fumiya Toyama
bio: I'm a software engineer specializing in backend development, and in our company, much of the development is done within Kubernetes clusters.

name: Juan Felipe Reyes Marlés
bio: I am a Lead DevOps Engineer and Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) specializing in AWS, Azure, Infrastructure as Code, and Kubernetes. I thrive on tackling intricate challenges by crafting and integrating cutting-edge solutions within the CNCF ecosystem. My passion lies in pushing the boundaries of what's possible in cloud-native technologies.

name: Vivek Velso
bio: Vivek Velso is a recognized CNCF Kubestronaut and open-source advocate who holds all five core Kubernetes certifications. He actively contributes to the cloud-native ecosystem by developing and sharing open-source AI and infrastructure projects, such as agentic frameworks and serverless CDK tools. Beyond his technical contributions, he serves as an AWS AI Hero and community mentor, frequently speaking at events to help organizations bridge the gap between cloud-native architecture and scalable AI systems.

name: Baudrin Tanguy
bio: Staff Engineer actually working for Sfeir, talking occasionally on Sfeir Lunch meetup. Writer at Sfeir.dev. I'm also giving trainings around Cloud Technologies.

name: El Mahdi Khachafi
bio: DevOps engineer with a strong focus on cloud-native technologies and Kubernetes. I work on designing, operating, and securing scalable, reliable platforms using modern DevOps and SRE practices. I am passionate about automation, continuous learning

name: Phanindra Sangers
bio: Hello, I’m Phanindra Sangers, passionate about cloud technologies and always eager to explore the inner workings of various tools. I have a strong interest in Kubernetes and other DevOps-related technologies, constantly seeking to deepen my understanding of how they function behind the scenes. Hit me up for any collaborations!

name: Volodymyr Pokhyla
bio: I am DevOps/Cloud engineer with over 20+ years of extensive experience in the IT-sphere taking on both engineering and leading roles. CFCN Kubestronaut, AWS Golden Jacket

name: Chris Finn
bio: Senior DevOps Engineer with a solid foundation in application development. I have a keen interest in all things Kubernetes, with a specific focus on observability and resource optimisation. Using Infrastructure as Code (Terraform), CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps), and GitOps tools (Flux CD) to apply DevOps best practices and develop cloud-native platforms for enterprise environments. I also contribute as an author and subject matter expert in delivering internal Kubernetes training. Microsoft Certified: DevOps Engineer Expert.

name: FréDéRic Klein
bio: I’m a Senior Solutions Architect at Red Hat with over 20 years of experience in IT and Networking, specializing in cloud-native technologies and modern applications. In my previous roles at Orange and VMware, I led large-scale transformation projects, designed resilient architectures, and automated deployments to drive technical innovation and optimize system performance. I share insights on my blog https://fklein.me and connect with professionals on https://fr.linkedin.com/in/fklein82 . Passionate about learning from the community, I stay engaged with the latest developments in the cloud-native world.

name: Pontus Ullgren
bio: Longtime Linux user 🐧, software developer, software architect and Platform Engineer. Constantly exploring new technologies and methodologies. I'm particularly interested by the challenges and opportunities within Identity and Access Management (IAM) and API management.  Favorite quote: sed/awk: changing the world one line at a time

name: Juan Carlos Salvador Garcia
bio: I'm a passionate about technology, innovation and always motivated to get new knowledge. I always enjoy researching topics about cloud, architecture, devops, cloud native, Kubernetes  and observability. I have the KCNA, KCSA, CKAD,CKA, CKS, AZ-700, AZ-305 certifications.
company: Banco de Crédito del Perú - BCP

name: Shivlal Sharma
bio: DevOps Engineer, Organiser @CNCG Ahmedabad, Public Speaker, Community Contributor, Kubernetes, AWS Certified Solution Architect (SAA-C03), HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate (003), CKA
