bio: I'm a Platform Engineer/SRE at RELEX Solutions.  My job, in a nutshell: making sure infrastructure stays out of the way so developers can focus on what actually matters. I live in the chaotic world of streaming data, cloud infrastructure, and distributed systems. What excites me most is figuring out how to make complex systems more approachable—without pretending the complexity isn't there. I'm endlessly curious, always learning, security and I genuinely enjoy sharing what I pick up along the way.

name: Eleni Grosdouli
bio: The go-to person for DevOps and Kubernetes Automation, with a passion for networking, security, endpoint management, and endpoint security, Eleni brings a diversity of experiences to her role as a DevOps Consulting Engineer at Cisco Systems.  Always eager to learn, she enjoys trying out the newest tech in a home lab and writing blog posts about her experiences. At this point in time, she is an active contributor to the ProjectSveltos. Beyond tech, she is all about sports and recently joined a local Rugby team.  Medium: https://medium.com/@eleni.grosdouli

name: Robert Markolwitz
bio: Back in 2009, when I was working in product management and marketing for a German IT company, I started to become heavily interested in what makes the internet work the way it does. I devoted a great deal of my free time to teach myself about networking and programming, developing just enough skills and confidence to eventually change careers and pursue software development for a living. Since 2012 I have earned my stripes as software engineer and for the last 6 years designed, created and maintained Kubernetes based architectures in multi-tenancy environments, crafted a fair share of CI/CD-Pipelines on various different platforms, managed application deployments (leveraging both, best-practices and, in hindsight, not-so-good practices), implemented automations to streamline workflows and overall helped development teams to ship their products faster and reliable, all while keeping a watchful eye on security. I do not hold any degree in CS but I am equipped with unstoppable drive to eventually „figure things out“. Currently I am working as a consultant, guiding and assisting clients to adopt cloud native technologies and to build, maintain and operate cloud (Azure) and hybrid infrastructures.

name: Gayan Pushpathilake
bio: I am Gayan Pushpathilake, an Architect specializing in DevOps and Middleware with an engineering background and a strong focus on cloud-native technologies. My expertise includes Kubernetes, container orchestration, and CNCF tools for building scalable and resilient systems. I actively contribute to knowledge sharing through internal workshops and community discussions, and I am passionate about promoting best practices in cloud-native adoption. You can find my technical insights on LinkedIn.

name: Ian Board
bio: A passionate SRE with a wealth of experience in various tech roles, Ian loves to gain deep technical understanding of the technology stacks he supports. Recently this has found him focusing on chaos engineering in Kubernetes clusters, to better understand common failure patterns and how best to mitigate against them.

name: Kamel Najjar
bio: I am a Solution Architect and Technical Director at Sopra Steria, leading cloud-native transformation and driving large-scale modernization initiatives across the energy, banking, and retail sectors. I design resilient, scalable, and secure cloud-native platforms that align technology strategy with business objectives, enabling organizations to gain agility, reliability, and long-term competitive advantage.

name: Koki Jean-David
bio: I am KOKI Jean-David, a DevOps and Cloud specialist with over seven years of experience, passionate about knowledge sharing, algorithms, mathematics, quantum physics, and computer science. Throughout my career, I have led teams and deepened my expertise in container orchestration, containerized environment security, cloud computing, and CI/CD processes. I actively contribute to the cloud-native community and share my insights on my GitLab repositories and on LinkedIn.

name: Ronit Banerjee
bio: I am Ronit Banerjee, a pre-final year Computer Science Engineering Undergraduate student. Along with my academics, I have maintained a strong portfolio being an Open Source Developer at the projects incubated and graduated from The Linux Foundation and Cloud Native Computing Foundation. My enthusiasm with DevOps and Cloud Native helped me to secure scholarships like Travel Funding Scholarship and Dan Kohn Scholarship by The Linux Foundation and Cloud Native Computing Foundation to attend flagship conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon + Open Source Summit China 2023 at Shanghai and Open Source Summit Europe 2022 at Dublin. I also worked as an Open Source Developer in Google Summer of Code (GSoC) at DBpedia Association. Apart from my technical experience, I got my hands dirty in fostering the Cloud Native Ecosystem. I am also the Chapter Lead at Cloud Native Hooghly which is an official CNCF Chapter listed amongst 40 such clubs in the Asia Pacific Region. Right now, with the proliferation of the Cloud Native Tools, I successfully turned the chapter to be the fastest growing in the entire Eastern India, catering 250+ Active members. I am also a Sig Contribex's Comms Volunteer for Kubernetes Contributors Summit North America 2023 at Chicago, United States of America primarily looking into the “Call for Proposal” emails and notifying about the updates to the KubeCon co-located event attendees. I have significant experience in pitching tech products to Venture Capitalists and have successfully secured grants from Government of India twice for my deep-tech startup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always wanted to boost Cloud Native Ecosystem in my locality whereas I felt there is a gap of awareness. Being an ambassador, it would really help me to reach areas where I couldn't do by myself just being a CNCG chapter lead of Hooghly. With CNCF's support it would be possible.

name: Md. Tanjin Alam
bio: I am a Software Engineer exploring CNCF technologies, with experience across multiple domains including software engineering, blockchain, and machine learning. I have worked as a mentor for Hyperledger: Learning Tokens for Hyperledger Community Building. I am deeply curious and always eager to learn new technologies, with hands-on experience building highly scalable software using industry best practices. My goal is to build practical, real-world solutions that create meaningful impact.

name: Nathan Morrissey
bio: I come from a full-stack background, starting out with building progressive web apps in Angular before diving deeper into the platform and infrastructure side. These days, I’m leading a small team responsible for all Integration and Test activities on a training platform.  Outside of work, you’ll find me in my ever-expanding homelab — which seems to be outgrowing its 22U rack faster than I can keep up.

name: Andy Wu

With a commitment to continuous learning and practical application, I aim to provide precise and innovative solutions. Embracing the spirit of exploration, I remain eager to uncover new opportunities and push the boundaries of cloud-native technologies.

I also have a strong interest in Kubernetes and its ecosystem, including ArgoCD and Grafana.

Outside of SRE work I am a passionate ultrarunner/rock climber who finds balance in nature.

name: Noe Thalheim
bio: Passionate troubleshooter, diving deep into complex systems to untangle and improve them.

Additionally, I possess strong skills in Elastic Stack for observability and security.  My work in the fintech and energy tech industries has given me a deep understanding of industry-specific challenges and solutions.  Passionate about CNCF projects, I continuously seek innovative solutions to enhance my skills.

name: Anil Sahu
bio: Cloud Specialist

name: Rodolfo Rivera López
bio: From the very moment I installed Red Hat 9 (without the Enterprise Edition), I knew open source was my calling. The challenge, the deep-seated need for knowledge to understand, the pure satisfaction of making something work, and then the drive to achieve much more—these forged my vision of technology. For me, every technical challenge is a designed opportunity to learn, execute, and iterate (not always in that order), a cycle that has given me powerful tools to deconstruct complex systems. This is my proven methodology for mastering what fascinates me most, a journey that has taken me through realms like GNU/Linux and BSD system administration, fullstack development, DevOps/Automation, Security and ultimately, the Kubernetes universe.  I believe technology defines us. Therefore, creating and applying it in the best possible way is, at its core, a contribution to a better society. This is why my commitment to learning is absolute and has no expiration date. It is the foundation from which I strive to reach the giants who built this wonderful open-source world, sharing whatever I can in return. Collaboration has proven to be the most viable path to the unprecedented; now is the time for all of us to contribute.

name: Sanjay Nithish K S

My core expertise includes Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and Bicep, CI/CD automation with GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps, and implementing DevSecOps and FinOps best practices. I have led initiatives around landing zones, policy-driven governance, container security, and Kubernetes-based workloads in regulated environments. I am passionate about building reliable, production-grade cloud-native systems.

name: Silas Bechet

My career has focused on helping development teams move faster and safer by building resilient infrastructure, automating delivery pipelines, and reducing operational overhead.

I specialize in DevOps and cloud-related technologies, like CI/CD, developer tools, Terraform, AWS, etc., and I blog on DevOps/DevSecOps.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am truly excited and honored to be an ambassador for the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF). This opportunity allows me to contribute to the promotion and adoption of cutting-edge cloud-native technologies, which play a crucial role in shaping the future of software development and infrastructure. Being an ambassador also connects me with a vibrant, passionate community of professionals who share insights and experiences, fostering collaboration and innovation. I am genuinely grateful for this chance to learn from others and help drive the industry forward, making a meaningful impact on the cloud-native ecosystem.

I enjoy creating standards, driving platform strategy, and building tooling that lets engineering teams move faster with fewer surprises.

location: Charoen Sin, Charoen Sin District, Sakon Nakhon, Thailand

I specialize in designing pipelines that not only move data efficiently but also ensure its quality and reliability. I'm always excited to connect with fellow professionals to share insights and explore innovative approaches to data integration challenges.

He has written a number of articles for IDPro (https://idpro.org/) and is currently on the editorial committee.  When not diving headlong into work projects, he loves to tinker with electronics and read. He currently works as a Federal Principal Architect for Radiant Logic.

His passion lies in assisting enterprises in adopting cloud-native practices and tools to enhance their agility, scalability, and resiliency.

When I'm not diving into the latest tech innovations, you'll find me traveling, meeting new people, and making friends along the way. Looking forward to connecting with you!

An active community contributor, Davi participates in KCD Porto and AWS User Group Lisbon since moving to Portugal, previously supporting OSS communities in Rio de Janeiro. He has contributed to several CNCF projects, primarily through documentation improvements and issue triage, demonstrating his commitment to advancing cloud-native technologies.

name: Carlos Sancho
bio: Software Engineer and Tech Lead at Redshift Global, with over 10 years of experience. With a strong focus on Kubernetes, security and Cloud Native approach.

name: Iceber Gu

I also created Clusterpedia and promoted its contribution to the CNCF Sandbox project. I organized KCD Beijing and KCD Shenzhen in China, and have been a speaker at Kubecon and many other events. I maintain my blog at https://icebergu.com/ and when I have time I share my thoughts on technology!Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am delighted to be a CNCF Ambassador. I am very grateful to CNCF for recognizing my work in the cloud native community. The influence of being a CNCF Ambassador allows me to better promote cloud-native technologies and the CNCF projects, make it easier to organize cloud-native events, and attract more people to participate in cloud-native construction. I will make more contributions to CNCF as an ambassador!

name: Kris Musard

Always eager to grow, learn, and embrace the next challenge in the ever-evolving world of cloud and DevSecOps. https://medium.com/@lhziim

name: Dheeraj Naithani

name: Jamal Ait Ahmed

Today, I build, optimize and secure OpenShift and Kubernetes environments where reliability is essential. Along the way, I earned my Red Hat Certified Architect certification and completed the CNCF journey to become a Kubestronaut.  I don’t see certifications as the end goal. They are a confirmation of my expertise and a way to keep my knowledge up to date in a rapidly changing landscape.  I don’t know everything. Nobody does. But when I encounter something I don’t know, I dive in, figure it out and make sure I understand it. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and improve.  Automation first. Security first. Always learning. Always building. Always improving.

Skilled in a wide array of cutting-edge technologies, including cloud platforms (GCP, AWS, Azure), containerization and orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker), infrastructure as code (Terraform), CI/CD (GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Harness), configuration management (Ansible), monitoring and logging, code quality (SonarQube), version control (Git, GitHub, Bitbucket), automation scripting (Python, Linux Shell Scripting), service mesh (Istio), and deployment automation (Helm Charts, Harness, Jenkins). My expertise lies in architecting scalable, resilient, and secure cloud-native environments while driving automation initiatives to streamline development workflows. Passionate about leveraging emerging technologies to enhance operational efficiency, accelerate time-to-market, and deliver exceptional user experiences. Let's connect to explore opportunities for collaboration and innovation in the ever-evolving landscape of DevOps and cloud technologies.

Known for his deep technical expertise and a people-centered approach, he bridges the gap between technology and collaboration to deliver impactful, high-quality solutions.

I am particularly interested in the challenge of making secure infrastructure simple enough for teams to use every day.

In this role, he oversees the strategy and development of Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) and Amazon Elastic Container Registry (ECR), helping organizations successfully run containerized applications at scale in the cloud.

Today I help managing Kube infrastructures with CNCF principles and help projects jumping in the band waggon.

Improving system reliability is close to my heart, and I enjoy collaborating with DevOps teams and IT architects to drive innovation forward.

name: Paul Balogh
bio: Paul is a Software Architect and Developer Advocate. In his many roles, he has encouraged developers and testers alike to "shift left," bringing reliability testing earlier in the software development process. As a software engineer/architect for over 20 years in varying languages, he now focuses more on the Go programming language. Active in the community, he organizes the St. Louis chapter of Cloud Native Community Groups and the StLGo Meetup group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a technologist, I became enamored with cloud-native technologies as soon as I was exposed. Events like KubeCon only supercharged my excitement for the overall CNCF project landscape. As a CNCF Ambassador, I'm hoping my enthusiasm for all things cloud-native will spread to those I meet at local events. I feel this platform will allow me to become more engaged as an advocate for CNCF projects, helping the community to gain more knowledge of opportunities and to encourage more active roles in open source contributions.

name: Alisan Stoll
bio: I am a software engineer captivated by the power and intricate orchestration of Kubernetes, drawn to the ever-evolving growth of the CNCF ecosystem and its vibrant community."

name: August Brzuzy
bio: Cloud-native engineer with extensive experience in DevOps, Platform Engineering, and Data Engineering, with a strong focus on IT infrastructure. Passionate about enabling organizations to adopt scalable, secure, and efficient solutions—primarily through the application of CNCF technologies in cloud environments.

name: Ayman Zahran
bio: AWS/Azure/GCP/VMWare/Kubernetes Cloud/DevOps/Platform/SRE Freelancer || AWS Community Builder || AWS Certification SME || 11x AWS || 5x Azure || 4x VMware || Kubestronaut(5x Kubernetes) || 3x Hashicorp

name: Kasper Borg Nissen
bio: Co-founder of the Nordic meetup alliance, Cloud Native Nordics, where he also organize the Aarhus group, and tries to help the nordic community as much as possible, e.g. setting up KCDs and more. Kasper also serves as Co-Chair for KubeCon+CloudNativeCon EU/NA 2024/2025. He works as a Developer Relations Engineer at Dash0, previously at Lunar where he in the past has shared many experiences with Cloud Native tech at meetups and conferences. Kasper wrote his master thesis, “KubeCloud - A Small Scale Cloud Computing Environment”, on how Universities could leverage small-scale cloud environments built out of Raspberry Pis running Kubernetes.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador, because it's a great community of like-minded people. I hope to meet and learn from the other awesome Ambassadors, and help grow the adoption of Cloud Native technologies even more here in the Nordics.

name: Prithvi Raj
bio: Prithvi Raj is working as a Community Manager & Developer Advocate at Mirantis and is leading the community efforts for the Open Source Program Office at Mirantis including the k0s project, k0smortron, and the other OSS projects Mirantis is contributing to. He previously led the LitmusChaos project community, the CNCF incubating project based on Cloud-Native Chaos Engineering, and has helped scale a community of 3000+ folks from scratch. He is also a CNCF Ambassador and has closely worked with the CNCF community to run LFX Mentorship programs and helped build a Chaos Engineering community in the CNCF ecosystem. He has worked on global events, conferences, and meetups such as Chaos Carnival, Kubernetes Community Days Bengaluru & Chennai, CNCF Kubernetes Chaos Engineering Meetups, and more to help grow various communities in the DevOps ecosystem. He started his journey in 2020 at MayaData and has been passionately helping build the Chaos Engineering community since, being part of ChaosNative and previously Harness. Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to represent the k0s as a CNCF ambassador and to also work with fellow ambassadors in helping curate more resources, events, and opportunities for the broader community. As an ambassador, I hope to take forward the cloud-native values amongst various folks keen on joining the ecosystem. I also look forward to learning a lot on this journey from my fellow ambassadors.

name: Sameer Mene
bio: A Cloud Solution Architect based in Pune, India, with a passion for cloud-native technologies, DevOps practices, and automation. Guided by the mantra, "If you do it more than twice, automate it!" I focus on simplifying workflows and building resilient systems. With expertise in Java, Go, and Python, I have designed and implemented solutions for Kubernetes cluster management, service mesh, operators, IAM, storage and edge computing. I specialize in crafting cloud-agnostic solutions, leveraging tools like Terraform and Pulumi to automate deployments and streamline operations across diverse environments. With a solid background in the telecom domain and a keen interest in emerging technologies such as 5G, my expertise also extends to AI, large language models (LLMs), and the dynamic landscape of Web 3.0.

name: Sebastian Prokesch
bio: Sebastian is a technology enthusiast and DevOps engineer with a strong technical background and a communication-driven approach focused on ensuring the reliability and performance of complex systems. He believes that in a rapidly evolving IT landscape, real expertise is built through hands-on practice rather than theory alone. His work centers on designing, building, and operating Kubernetes-based platforms that enable development teams to ship software reliably and at scale. He focuses on automation, clear abstractions, and operational excellence to deliver sustainable, production-ready platforms.

name: Yin Zhang
bio: Yin Zhang is an experienced cloud platform engineer based in Melbourne, with over 10 years of hands-on expertise in AWS and a strong focus on cloud-native technologies. Throughout his career, he has developed deep expertise in Kubernetes orchestration and container security, with a passion for building resilient, scalable platforms. As a strong advocate for cloud security best practices, Yin prioritises implementing defense-in-depth strategies across infrastructure, from secure container pipelines to comprehensive access controls and network security. He is committed to advancing the Kubernetes ecosystem and sharing knowledge with the cloud-native community while continuously exploring innovative approaches to secure, efficient platform engineering

name: Yan Zakharau
bio: Kubernetes is my passion — I love experimenting, learning, and pushing the limits of cloud-native tech. I enjoy helping others navigate the ecosystem and sharing practical insights. Thrilled to be part of Kubestronaut and grow together with the community.

name: Erin Colvin
bio: Erin is an experienced Event Manager with a specific expertise in Tradeshows and Conferences. She has a passion for strategic event marketing, strong vendor relationships, and delivering exceptional experiences. She earned her Bachelor's degree in Communications and Business at the University of Utah, and began her career in sales and marketing. She managed 20+ tradeshows annually for a CPG company, before moving to the agency side where she mastered her Project Management and Customer Services skills managing experiential marketing campaigns for clients like Amazon Fresh and Kodiak Cakes. She recently received her Master's degree in Strategic Communication and is excited to utilize this knowledge to bring more research, data and strategy into her projects and events.

name: Edwin Harley Bermúdez Cuervo
bio: I'm a passionate DevOps and Site Reliability Engineer advancing cloud-native technologies and platform engineering. As a Kubestronaut with multiple CNCF certifications, I bring deep expertise in Kubernetes security, operations, and cloud-native application delivery. I design resilient, scalable, and highly automated platforms across hybrid and multi-cloud environments, leveraging Kubernetes, GitOps, Infrastructure as Code, and observability to empower high-performing teams. Committed to strengthening Colombia's cloud-native community, I actively foster collaboration, share knowledge, and champion local CNCF initiatives.

Now heavily focused on cloud-native technologies, I specialize in building scalable, production-grade systems using Kubernetes, Istio, Prometheus, ArgoCD, and other CNCF projects. I'm passionate about leveraging modern infrastructure practices to solve real-world challenges.

name: Ei Mon Win Aung
bio: I am passionate about searching for solutions to meet customer and user requirements, and I began studying this cloud-native journey in 2023.

name: Javier Guzman

Looking forward to collaborate in the development of Kubernetes operators or internals.

name: Ehsan Rafiei Azad

I’m a CNCF Kubestronaut (KCNA, KCSA, CKAD, CKA, CKS) and specialize in Kubernetes platform engineering, Infrastructure as Code, and cloud-native systems across AWS and Azure, plus service mesh with Istio and GitOps with Argo CD. My focus is operational excellence: SLOs/SLIs, incident response, observability (Prometheus/Grafana/ELK), and performance/cost optimization that helps teams ship reliably at scale.

name: Aakansha Priya

In the past, she has been a Community Manager at Hashnode & WeMakeDevs, experienced in working efficiently in groups as well as individually.  As a frontend dev turned DevOps explorer, loves sharing tech insights, and views on learning & building in public, blogging & building personal brand. Aakansha, a reader and speaker, paints, sings, and loves spontaneous travel. She has spoken at various conferences about DevOps and the human side of tech. Catch her at meetups & conferences!

name: Tibo Beijen
bio: I am an IT professional with over 20 years of experience in software engineering, architecture, and DevOps.My Kubernetes journey started in 2017. Always having leaned towards open source, I have developed a deep appreciation of the CNCF ecosystem.  Ultimately my masters in Industrial Design Engineering shines through: The tech and the focus on stakeholders and end-users, resulting in innovation and the crafting of products that offer a great experience.

name: Patrick Eichler
bio: I'm Patrick, a freelancer who specializes in site reliability engineering and Kubernetes. Through reading IT books, IT-Bloqs, and autodidactic studies, I constantly broaden my horizon with state-of-the-art expert knowledge and modern technical skills, which I not only use in my daily work and personal projects, but also blog about and teach to other people.

name: Rainui Ly
bio: Engineer, cloud-native computing enthusiast, open-source advocate, fan of KubeCon+CloudNativeCon. Speciality in security, software development and DevOps/Platform practices. The best process is the learning one.

name: Kevin (Zefeng) Wang

Kevin has contributed critical enhancements to Kubernetes, led the incubation of the KubeEdge, Volcano, Karmada projects in CNCF, actively participated in the TOC, and played a critical role in building and supporting the growth of CNCF technologies and the CNCF community in the globe.

name: Nikita Romm
bio: I'm a DevOps engineer with experience working with a wide range of tools, including Kubernetes, Terraform, multi-cloud systems, and CI/CD pipelines. I have a strong background in Linux and scripting, enabling me to automate processes and optimize workflows efficiently. My expertise extends to many other tools and technologies, and I'm constantly expanding my skill set to stay ahead in the field. I enjoy tackling complex problems and applying innovative solutions to improve system performance and efficiency. I'm committed to applying innovative solutions to every challenge and driving impactful results.

name: Serhii Plesovskykh
bio: Hello everyone, I'm a cloud architect engineer passionate about Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. I spend 1.5 to 2 hours every day learning something new to stay sharp and grow my skills. Over the years, I've earned all certifications from AWS, GCP, and Microsoft, some of which have already been recertified for the second time, which reflects my hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure, automation, and security. I enjoy building scalable, resilient systems and constantly look for ways to improve and evolve in this fast-moving industry.

name: Chase Christensen
bio: Chase Christensen is a Principal Customer Engineer at Wiz, where he helps organizations secure cloud-native and ML workloads. His journey into MLOps began through the Kubeflow community, where he went on to chair both the 2025 Kubeflow Summit in Atlanta (https://colocatedeventsna2025.sched.com/event/28FLh/kubeflow-summit-welcome-+-opening-remarks-kubeflow-summit-a-community-big-enough-for-everyone-chase-christensen-wiz)and the Kubeflow Virtual Symposium (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t0vfJY267f0&list=PLj6h78yzYM2MCEJPmVgUZsEx7lFjqh8BC&index=5).  Along the way, he authored the Linux Foundation’s Introduction to AI/ML Toolkits with Kubeflow course, helping practitioners bridge Kubernetes expertise with machine learning practice. Chase also founded the Applied Machine Learning Collective of the Rockies (https://amlcoftherockies.org/), a Denver-based community focused on advancing hands-on AI/ML collaboration. He shares his thoughts on MLOps and open-source adoption as a contributor to the Evil Tux blog (https://eviltux.com/2025/06/17/the-kubeflow-blog-series/).

name: Jonah Kowall
bio: Jonah Kowall, computer scientist and open-source advocate. As a member of the founding team and technical leadership committee for OpenSearch a Linux Foundation project, Maintainer of Jaeger a CNCF graduated project for distributed tracing. Contributor to OpenTelemetry. A technical leader across startups and large enterprises specialized in operations, security, and performance. Led Gartner research on monitoring. Product leadership at AppDynamics, Cisco (post-acquisition), Kentik, Logz.io, Aiven, and is currently the Senior Vice President product and design at Paessler, the monitoring company.

name: Cristian Alloggio
bio: I am Cristian, DevOps Engineer, Solution Architect and a passionate advocate of technology innovation with a strong focus on DevOps technologies and best practices.What started as curiosity turned into passion... and now it's a mission!I am always curious to learn new technologies related to the cloud ecosystem, especially containerization.  I actively participate in the local technology community, sharing insights and experiences through collaborative projects and knowledge sharing sessions.

My work in fintech has focused on enhancing security, visibility, and observability through robust monitoring, logging, and tracing solutions, ensuring scalable and secure application deployments. I am passionate about advancing cloud-native technologies and fostering best practices in the DevOps and Kubernetes ecosystems.

name: Preet Pal
bio: Cloud Engineer at Cisco

Active contributor on dev.to AWS and Microsoft tag moderation, AWS Builder Center technical blogs, and recognized in AWS PartyRock Hackathon. Driven by democratizing cloud-native education across South Asia, he bridges students with global ecosystems while promoting inclusive tech communities.

My work spans Kubernetes operations, CI/CD engineering, and cloud cost optimization, where I’ve helped improve reliability, streamline deployments, and reduce costs. I enjoy research and innovation, mentoring teams, and leading workshops to share knowledge. Outside of work, I’m a normal guy who enjoys learning new technologies and experimenting with creative cloud-native solutions. also, I Love Cats.

name: Jason Khoi
bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer transitioned from more than a decade old of software developer role. As a result, I know Cloud infrastructure and software development equally well. I love the philosophy of Kubernetes and have a passion of using CNCF Prometheus for infrastructure and application observability & monitoring.

name: Nguyen Doan
bio: Hello, greetings to those who are passionate about cloud-native technologies. Like everyone else, I really enjoy exploring and delving into new things within CNCF tools, then applying these insights to my company and sharing them with others.

name: Amr Muhammed Mahmoud
bio: Enthusiastic DevOps/Platform Engineer dedicated to delivering innovative and scalable solutions that drive organizational growth. With expertise in CI/CD pipeline architecture, Kubernetes management, cloud-native infrastructure, observability, and security integration, I ensure secure and efficient software delivery. I leverage cutting-edge technologies, particularly within the Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems, to drive operational excellence and foster innovation. Passionate about collaborating with highly skilled teams to lead impactful digital transformation initiatives, bridging the gap between development and operations to achieve efficiency, resilience, and continuous improvement.

name: João Santos
bio: I’m a DevSecOps Engineer at Scalabit.dev, where I help teams ship software securely and efficiently through automation, CI/CD, IaC, Kubernetes, and Security best practices.  In 2023, I transitioned from a 7-year career as a personal trainer to tech — with no prior experience and a tight schedule (full-time job + wife + 2 kids!). I did it all using free resources, discipline, and determination.  Now, I’m passionate about helping others make the same leap into tech — especially those seeking flexibility, growth, and better work-life balance. 💻🌍 👉 ebook.jocasantos.com

name: Liz Rice
bio: Liz Rice is Chief Open Source Officer with eBPF specialists Isovalent, now part of Cisco, and original creators of the Cilium cloud native networking, security and observability project. She is the author of Container Security, and Learning eBPF, both published by O'Reilly. She was Chair of the CNCF's Technical Oversight Committee in 2019-2022, and Co-Chair of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon in 2018.She has a wealth of software development, team, and product management experience from working on network protocols and distributed systems, and in digital technology sectors such as VOD, music, and VoIP. When not writing code, or talking about it, Liz loves riding bikes in places with better weather than her native London, competing in virtual races on Zwift, and making music under the pseudonym Insider Nine.

name: Patryk Wieczorek
bio: I am passionate about IT and I run the most dedicated study group and stream daily how I study at 5 AM at https://www.youtube.com/@thefiveamstudy

name: Ebin Babu
bio: I'm Ebin Babu, an engineer turned event organizer on a mission to ignite the cloud-native revolution in Kerala. As a passionate evangelist in the open-source world, I juggle multiple roles – from heading operations at Stackgenie.io to co-founding devopsmalayalam.io and spearheading the CNCG Trivandrum chapter. My overarching aim? To cultivate a vibrant, collaborative cloud-native community in our dynamic state.  Throughout my journey as an organizer, I've orchestrated over 100 online and offline events, fostering connections and exchanging knowledge among fellow cloud-native enthusiasts. It's been incredibly rewarding to witness this community flourish and expand. Together, we're nurturing a shared passion for open-source, Linux, Kubernetes, and the ever-evolving world of Istio.  What truly fuels my drive is the diverse tapestry of minds within our community – constantly pushing boundaries and shaping the future of technology. I derive immense satisfaction from uniting these innovators and providing a platform for collaboration, ideation, and collective growth.

name: Wouter Damman
bio: Hi! I’m Wouter, a Cloud Engineer from the Netherlands with a passion for Kubernetes and the cloud-native world. My interest in technology and automation led me down this path, allowing me to explore how the cloud-native world fits together. both at work and in my homelab. I enjoy solving problems through automation and turning ideas into reliable, maintainable solutions.

name: Matthew Robinson
bio: Matt has been doing DevOps since the mid-90's and for more than twenty years has been working in the higher education sector, ensuring students and researchers have access to the resources they need. Matt was introduced to containers almost a decade ago and a cloud migration project in 2000 allowed a move to Kubernetes. Kubernetes allowed a complex migration to be completed quickly and painlessly. As new challenges need solving, he often finds the solution exists in another CNCF project (Argo, Helm, Prometheus, OPA etc). Matt is excited about the future of cloud native and the future of CNCF projects.

name: Mohsin Nisar
bio: Experienced professional with expertise in Kubernetes, DevOps, MLOps, and cloud technologies. Skilled in designing scalable, automated solutions to optimize workflows, boost operational efficiency, and foster innovation in cloud-native ecosystems.

name: Gary Chang
bio: Hi, I am Gary, a Toronto-based DevOps Engineer specializing in enterprise infrastructure modernization. As a dedicated Kubestronaut, I am passionate about orchestrating containerized microservices and implementing GitOps workflows with Argo CD and Terraform to build resilient cloud-native platforms. I thrive on automating complex self-service pipelines and am always eager to experiment with new CNCF projects to solve real-world scaling challenges.

name: Raymond Tan
bio: As a cloud-native architect, I'm driven to build resilient and lasting systems by blending timeless architectural principles with pragmatic, automated operations. I have a discerning eye for quality design, always aiming for solutions that are as scalable and performant as they are simple to maintain and evolve. Ultimately, my philosophy is to pursue an elegant efficiency, crafting distributed systems that empower developers and deliver enduring value to the community.

name: Sarawut Klangphukhiao
bio: I'm a Senior DevOps Consultant with over 7 years of experience in cloud-native technologies, including Kubernetes, OpenShift, and the full CNCF stack. I'm an active contributor in local DevOps and cloud communities in Thailand, and I enjoy mentoring others preparing for CNCF certifications.

name: Venkatesh Venugopal
bio: A seasoned Senior DevOps Engineer with 15 years of experience building the backbone of mission-critical systems. My expertise lies in transforming complex cloud-native ecosystems into high-performance, secure, and cost-efficient engines of growth. Beyond mastering Kubernetes orchestration and CI/CD automation, I am dedicated to the art of SRE—ensuring system reliability while driving the continuous improvement that keeps modern enterprises ahead of the curve. I believe in leading by example, mentoring teams, and architecting the future of scalable infrastructure.

name: Matthew Rodgers
bio: I am a Senior Software Engineer with a passion for cloud-native technologies and the Kubernetes ecosystem. I have experience developing modern applications, migrating legacy systems, and deploying tools that enhance workflows within Kubernetes environments. I enjoy solving complex challenges around scalability and reliability while writing clean, maintainable code. Excited to keep learning, I look forward to deepening my expertise and contributing to the Kubernetes community.

name: Omar Elfarsaoui
bio: I’m Omar, a passionate cloud-native enthusiast with a strong love for Kubernetes, DevOps culture, and security. I enjoy building, breaking, and securing systems while continuously learning through hands-on projects, books, and the open-source community.  I code for fun, automate everything I can, and I’m deeply interested in how infrastructure, security, and software intersect in modern cloud environments.  Currently focused on:  Kubernetes & Cloud Native ecosystems DevOps & Platform Engineering Infrastructure as Code Cloud & Container Security

name: David Gondor
bio: I'm David Gondor, a Test engineer with a strong focus on quality and reliability. I'm transitioning into DevOps, expanding my skills in automation, cloud, and CI/CD. I’m passionate about building efficient, scalable systems that support fast and reliable software delivery.
location: Alphen aan den Rijn, Zuid-Holland, Netherlands

name: Hyungchan Cho
bio: Hello, I’m currently leading ALM and DevOps engineering initiatives at Hyundai Mobis, where I focus on driving innovation in areas such as CI/CD and cloud-native transformation.  For over three years, my group and I have designed, built, and operated a hybrid cloud infrastructure that seamlessly integrates an on-premises Kubernetes cluster with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) — achieving an optimal balance of scalability and cost efficiency.  My core expertise lies in Kubernetes operations, security, and observability, ensuring resilient and transparent platform management across complex environments.  I’m also deeply passionate about Generative AI technologies, particularly AI-powered code assistants, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — exploring how these can further elevate developer productivity and system intelligence.

name: Joel G.
bio: I love to develop secure, reliable and scalable services while automating redundant tasks to keep my productivity high. I really enjoy learning new things to keep up with the future and deepen my knowledge to a level where I am able to teach about it.

name: Marcus Ross
bio: In the past ten years, I have been addicted to the cloud (AWS/Azure/OpenTofu) and Kubernetes, helping enterprise customers to achieve their solutions. Always try to align business requirements with technology. I share my real-world experience from projects as an official Linuxfoudnation Kubernetes Trainer, AWS ChampionTrainer, and MCT.

name: Uroš Hercog
bio: I'm a Site Reliability Engineer at Veza, the Identity Security company, primarily focusing on tools like Kubernetes, Prometheus, and Cilium. My expertise lies in optimizing infrastructure resilience and automating deployment processes to improve reliability across dynamic environments.

name: Adhi Sutandi
bio: Senior DevOps Engineer based in Zürich, specializing in helping organizations scale their IT systems and achieve N times developer productivity. I focus on building solutions that directly fit business goals and are designed to last, based on solid system designs. My toolkit includes key CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Flux, Helm, Prometheus, and provisioning tools like OpenTofu and Sops. I share my occasional insights and technical guides on my blog: https://medium.com/@sanadhis.

name: Jacopo Binosi
bio: I first encountered Linux at university, and from that moment, I never looked back. It was then that I realized Linux would be my lifelong "familiar."  I enjoy solving problems and making things work the way I want. This, combined with my passion for Linux, led me to a career as a Systems Engineer after earning my Bachelor's degree in Physics. Time flew by, and I transitioned to the Cloud... and to Neovim (I’ve always been a daily Vim user and a fan of CLI tools in general).  Today, I’m deeply into Kubernetes, but I still use Vim, Ubuntu, Debian, Arch, and occasionally work with some legacy infrastructure. Now that I’m a "Kubestronaut," I see this as a fresh starting point, without forgetting the tools that got me here.

name: Thibault Sourdin
bio: I’m a cloud engineer with 2 years of production experience on Kubernetes. My background includes embedded systems (building embedded Linux distributions), which shaped my interest in understanding systems beneath their abstractions. I enjoy diving into Kubernetes internals, Linux, networking, and security to design reliable infrastructures.

name: Anis Hannachi
bio: Platform & DevOps engineer with strong hands-on experience operating Kubernetes platforms using Helm and GitOps with Argo CD. I work across the full DevOps stack, from cluster operations and platform architecture to CI/CD, automation and observability. My background spans on-premise, hybrid, and cloud environments, where I focus on building scalable and maintainable Kubernetes-based systems. I enjoy designing platform architectures, automating workflows, and enabling teams to run production workloads efficiently.

name: Peter West
bio: I've worked in the IT industry for 35 years and have held a variety of roles during this time.  More recently i've worked extensively with Kubernetes and AWS whilst automating using languages such as Python and Go.  Although I don't currently use Kubernetes as part of my day to day work I find it incredibly interesting so keep my hand in by running clusters at home which are then used for various projects.
location: Cleethorpes, North East Lincolnshire, United Kingdom

name: Phoo Phoo Thet Lyar Tun
bio: I am a cloud-native architect specializing in CNCF technologies, multi-cloud infrastructure, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), Service Mesh (Istio and Cilium), Container Orchestration (Kubernetes) and GitOps. I attended KubeDay Singapore 2023, staying up to date with the latest trends in cloud-native computing.

name: Than Htike Phyo
bio: I am a Network Security professional specializing in secure enterprise and hybrid infrastructure design, with strong expertise in next-generation firewalls, SD-WAN, SASE, and Zero Trust architectures. I work extensively with cloud-native and Kubernetes environments, focusing on CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, service mesh, and cloud security integration. My experience spans secure campus networking, micro-segmentation, and integrating security into DevOps and container platforms. I am passionate about bridging traditional network security with modern cloud-native architectures. I regularly share insights and technical deep dives on infrastructure security and Kubernetes security best practices.

name: Momoko Murakami
bio: I am a Cloud Security Engineer specializing in designing, implementing, and operating security solutions for multi-cloud environments, including AWS, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes. I also leverage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for secure, automated deployments.

name: Tony Prawiro Hadimulyono
bio: Tony is a tech enthusiast who loves cloud computing, containers, DevOps, and open-source technologies. He works as a cybersecurity engineer, focusing on making cloud and DevOps platforms secure. In his job, Tony collaborates with others to create threat models, suggest security controls, and develop security monitoring use cases for SIEM systems.  Tony enjoys learning new things and has earned certifications from AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, ISC2 CSSLP, and CNCF/Linux Foundation Kubernetes. He is now learning about Machine Learning and Generative AI, with the goal of using AI to enhance the security of cloud and DevOps platforms.

name: Woravit Ariyakunatorn
bio: With over a decade of experience in IT, I specialize in cloud architecture, DevOps, and automation. I enjoy experimenting with Kubernetes and other cloud-native technologies both professionally and as a hobby. My interests include designing secure, scalable infrastructure and building modern solutions using AWS, Kubernetes, and serverless platforms. Outside of technology, I’m passionate about fishing and exploring the outdoors.

name: Amit Kumar Gupta
bio: Working in harmony as a Platform Engineer, leveraging Terraform & Kubernetes expertise to design and deliver secure, scalable, and reliable infrastructure on AWS Cloud. Supporting a well-established client by provisioning platform information systems, enabling Kubernetes-based workloads for seamless communication and operational efficiency. Beyond cloud-native engineering, I'm actively exploring Generative AI, continuously expanding my skillset to stay ahead in the evolving tech landscape. Pursuing industry-recognised certifications to solidify my expertise and stand out in a competitive field. Please follow me on https://www.linkedin.com/in/amitdctm/, https://github.com/amitdctm and https://gitlab.com/documentum-stack

name: Rajan Jha
bio: A seasoned technology leader with over a decade of expertise spanning diverse domains, including Banking, E-commerce, and Energy.  Bringing 10+ years of experience, I specialise in Platform Engineering, DevOps, and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), with a proven track record of designing, building, and managing resilient, scalable, and secure infrastructure and services. Starting my journey as Linux Systems Engineering, I gained a strong foundation in application lifecycles and modern IT Infrastructure management, enhancing my expertise in modern platform solutions.

name: Halil Ibrahim Bugol
bio: I started my career in a data center in Istanbul, Turkey. I have been interested in the CNCF world since 2018. Although my main areas are generally technical aspects, there were companies where I took part in community and product management. I am currently in the role of Co-Founder at Kubezy. Apart from the commercial side, we have been organizing events as Organizers at CNCG Istanbul since last year, together with my friends in Turkey. I am also a member of the KCD Türkiye team. I have a community role as Technology Evangelist at KubeSphere and KubeSphere Products.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am in love with community and all things community. I have met so many people since I first joined the CNCF community. It is a great pleasure to contribute to this community with people from all over the world who believe in the same goal. One goal and hundreds of people contributing to this goal. I think my biggest excitement in being an ambassador is making new friends and representing my community in the best way possible. Also, ambassador swags are great :)

name: Jay Koby
bio: Jay Koby brings more than a decade of experience working with a variety of organizations and technologies.  He currently works as a Senior Observability Architect at Grafana Labs.

name: Long Li
bio: I am an SRE. I began exploring cloud-native technologies in 2022 and successfully obtained CKA and CKS certifications. I have developed a strong passion for cloud-native technologies and am currently focusing on cloud-native DevOps practices.

name: Chimbu Chinnadurai
bio: Chimbu is a passionate senior cloud architect with a knack for solving complex cloud challenges.He has a proven track record of helping companies of all sizes leverage the power of Kubernetes and achieve greater scalability, agility, and resilience.He actively shares his expertise through insightful blog posts on his Medium page (https://chimbu.medium.com/) and by engaging with the Open Source and Cloud Native communities at industry conferences.

name: Roberth Strand
bio: I am a self-proclaimed "cloud automator", and I work at Sopra Steria based out of Oslo, Norway. I have been granted the title of Microsoft MVP for my work with Azure, and I have been an active contributor in the CNCF for which I earned the title Cloud Native Ambassador. I initiated Cloud Native Norway, a non-profit that works to build up and enable Cloud Native adoption and development in Norway. I also co-founded the Azure Cloud Native User Group, and founded the Norwegian PowerShell User Group. In the CNCF, I have been active for years in TAG App Delivery, and currently serves as co-chair for the TAG, maintainer on the OpenGitOps project, and as co-chair of the Platforms Working Group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: For many years, the CNCF has continued to impress me. The way that the foundation has supported the community and the projects is truly admirable. Even though I have other honorary titles, the CNCF Ambassador title is one that I value very much. While I don't do community work for the titles, I am happy for the recognition. I have big plans for my work in the CNCF, and I look forward to seeing how the ambassador program will help me achieve more for the community.Sessionize: https://sessionize.com/roberth-strand

name: Łukasz Amróz
bio: Frontend Developer that have been working with Kubernetes for the past three years. Interested in DevOps tools and technologies.

name: Artem Bortnikov
bio: Passionate software/infrastructure engineer. Keen on Kubernetes. In love with Golang and Rust. I love being on the cutting edge of technology and bringing things together that weren't originally meant to be together.

name: Kylash Mukund Rameshbapu
bio: I am currently working as a DevOps Engineer with over 4 years of combined experience in managing ETL workloads and deploying cloud applications using DevOps practices. Recently, I transitioned into the DevOps domain, where I have built hands-on expertise working with various Devops tools. Having the Kubestronaut credential demonstrates my commitment to continuous learning and my ability to adapt to new technologies. I am excited to leverage my skills and contribute to the Cloud Native community in the coming years.

name: Matteo Mortari
bio: I strongly believe Kubernetes is an awesome platform not only for cloud-native, app orchestration and workload, but especially for MLOps (Kubeflow) and in Containerization: not only for runnable images but also for broad OCI packaging and distribution (ORAS/Podman/Buildah) especially in relation to ML model assets and integration with signature, attestation. I contribute to Kubeflow, Oras, and other Open Source projects. I'm a co-organizer of the local JUG (JVM User Group) Milano, and I'm active in other Italian communities, often contributing my passion/hobby of live streaming and video recording.

name: Roland Rust
bio: With a background in software development and system architecture, I understand the importance of reliable and scalable infrastructure platforms. As a Site Reliability Engineer, I focus on building and operating highly available hybrid cloud environments. My expertise includes managing multiple Kubernetes clusters and diverse infrastructure components using infrastructure-as-code practices. I am dedicated to empowering development teams through efficient GitOps workflows and am passionate about monitoring and metrics to ensure optimal system performance and resiliency. I strive to bridge the gap between development and operations, driving continuous improvement across the organization.

name: Bob Killen
bio: Bob Killen is a Senior Technical Program Manager focusing on improving CNCF project governance processes and finding engaging ways to bring end users and maintainers together. He serves the Kubernetes project as a Steering Committee member and is a recently retired Contributor Experience Special Interest Group chair. Before joining the CNCF, Bob was a program manager at Google's Open Source Programs Office, where he guided and developed strategies for Google's open source projects. Bob has a history of bringing cloud and HPC/AI/ML together and is passionate about Academic Outreach. He is also an outspoken advocate of Open Source, Open Science, and pets of the furry 4-legged variety.

name: Viktar Mikalayeu
bio: SRE  , AWS solution architect , FinOps consultant,DevOps  , AWS Community Builder , Open-source

name: Igor Eulalio
bio: Container, Cloud and Kubernetes expert, organizer of KCD Brasil.Currently main contributor of Falco Talon, a project from the Falco ecosystem, aiming to provide open source automatic threat response for Kubernetes.

name: Katherine Hughes
bio: Katherine brings over a decade of experience in event management, with a strong background in planning and executing corporate and nonprofit conferences and events of all sizes across a wide range of industries and destinations. She has a proven track record in contract negotiation, event logistics, and production management. Detail-oriented and creativity-driven, Katherine thrives on juggling complex logistics, solving problems in real time, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, high-impact experiences. She’s always looking for ways to improve efficiency, elevate attendee engagement, and exceed expectations.

name: Arjun Kaushal
bio: Cloud consultant with 5+ years of experience migrating applications from on-prem to the cloud. Strong proficiency in Azure, and learned Kubernetes through multiple projects which took advantage of AKS. Passionate about DevOps, IaC, and Security, and always excited to learn more.

name: Fangel Emilio Colón Navarro
bio: Hello, I'm Fangel, I live in the Dominican Republic. My first steps using CNCF technologies began in 2020 when the technological world advanced significantly. As of today I have already achieved a lot of knowledge and my wishes are to continue in this wonderful world.

name: Karthi Palanisamy
bio: Karthi Palanisamy is a Senior Site Reliability Engineer with 12+ years of experience operating large-scale cloud-native platforms and production Kubernetes environments. He actively contributes to the Kubernetes ecosystem, including documentation contributions to the Kubernetes website and regular participation in SIG ContribEx meetings, with a growing focus on community enablement and contributor experience. His technical expertise spans Kubernetes operations, GitOps, Infrastructure as Code, and reliability automation, with real-world impact in platform governance and cloud cost optimization. Karthi is also an open-source contributor and writes about SRE and cloud-native operations on Medium (https://medium.com/@karthipalanisamy14).  His current interests include applying AI to improve Kubernetes reliability, SRE workflows, and operational decision-making in complex systems.

name: Kostiantyn Lysenko
bio: Platform Engineer with over six years of hands-on experience designing and building Kubernetes-native platforms, operators, admission webhooks, and infrastructure management tools. I specialize in CNCF technologies and work extensively with Argo Rollouts/CD, Crossplane, KCL, Rancher, and the Kubernetes Operator SDK across multi-cloud environments (GCP & AWS). I’m passionate about open source and actively exploring ways to contribute to the CNCF ecosystem, often prototyping tools and frameworks to simplify and scale platform engineering challenges.

name: Matija Popovic
bio: Matija is a cloud engineer and developer working with Kubernetes, Terraform, and Azure.

name: Anssi Järvinen
bio: Anssi is an experienced cloud and DevOps professional specializing in Azure, container technologies, and PaaS solutions, with solid skills in Azure networking.

name: Richard Kooij
bio: Astronomer turned Kubestronaut. Experienced in event driven, microservices architecture - all cloud-native.

name: Zoltán Lajos Kis
bio: System architect with 15+ years of experience in the telecom industry working with a diverse set of technologies through the complete software stack. Always learning and utilizing new technologies to understand trade-offs and benefits. Long history of building proof of concepts and gradually evolving them into product features or poducts.

name: Eralp Eryanılmaz
bio: Former IBMer and currently a Cloud Big Data System Engineer at GarantiBBVA Technology, I work extensively with the Hadoop ecosystem, Kafka, the ELK Stack, and MongoDB. I am passionate about managing and using container platforms. It is an honor for me to join the Kubestronaut family, also holding PCA and CAPA certifications, reflecting my commitment to continuous learning and growing within the cloud-based ecosystem.

name: Kenta Tada
bio:
Kenta Tada has worked as a Linux engineer and team lead for various organizations, specializing in Linux kernel tracing technologies and eBPF. He has contributed to the Linux kernel and the BPF Compiler Collection (BCC) to enhance kernel tracing infrastructure. He has also contributed to several CNCF-related projects, including serving as a reviewer for the system validator used in kubeadm preflight checks.

In 2023, he reviewed the Japanese translation of Learning eBPF, published by O'Reilly Japan. In 2024, he became an organizer of Cloud Native Community Japan (CNCJ), fostering open source culture in Japan. Additionally, he served as Co-Chair of KubeDay Japan 2024, playing a key role in shaping the event and promoting cloud-native technologies within the Japanese developer community.

Recognizing the growing importance of eBPF in cloud-native environments, Kenta founded the eBPF Japan Community for engineers across various industries to collaborate, share knowledge, and drive eBPF adoption in Japan.

name: Mohan Singh Bartwal
bio: I am passionate about infrastructure observability and data democratization(KPI/SLI/SLO). Skilled in consolidating and integrating observability data across organizational silos, enabling horizontal and vertical data sharing to empower data-driven decision-making. Led IT infrastructure teams, to drive cloud migration-transformations. Encouraged and mentored team to enhance operational excellence, deliver high availability, business continuity, and disaster recovery solutions. Member of the ISC2 and PMI BayArea local chapters.

name: Dekel Malul
bio: Hello from Israel 🇮🇱! Passionate about community collaboration, I actively contribute to the Kubernetes community by sharing insights on cloud-native technologies through my blog. I currently work as a DevOps Engineer, with a strong focus on providing Kubernetes assistance and guidance to Israeli startup companies.

name: Satoshi Uchida
bio: Software engineer focused on cloud-native and platform engineering, with deep interest in Kubernetes. I enjoy working at the boundary between infrastructure and application development, and value clear design, automation, and sustainable engineering practices.

name: Kansei Chiba
bio: I’m an infrastructure engineer who loves OSS such as Linux, Kubernetes, and Argo CD. I’m particularly interested in cloud-native infrastructure design and operational improvements, and I focus on building systems that are reliable and sustainable. Outside of work, I’m a passionate cyclist, applying the same mindset of long-term stability and safety to both technology and my hobbies. If you have any questions or challenges around infrastructure or OSS, feel free to reach out to me on LinkedIn anytime.

name: Raphaël Pinson
bio: Raphaël Pinson is a seasoned Solutions Architect with over 15 years of experience in the tech industry. He specializes in Kubernetes, Terraform, and Cilium, and currently works at Isovalent. Raphaël is passionate about bridging the gap between DevOps and Cloud Native communities, and he's excited to represent CNCF as a co-organizer of the Swiss Romandie (Lausanne chapter) CNCF meetup and regular speaker at industry events. Raphaël has contributed to various CNCF projects. His public work can be found at https://polywork.com/raphink.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to become a CNCF ambassador because it allows me to expand my impact and bring Cloud Native technology to a wider audience. By representing CNCF and bridging the gap between Cloud Native and other communities, I hope to further advance our industry's growth and adoption. It's an honor to be part of a community of talented and passionate advocates who are driving innovation and making a difference in the tech world.

name: Joseph Sandoval
bio: I'm Joseph Sandoval, a Principal Product Manager at Adobe, where I lead product strategy for our internal developer platform. I've been in tech for over 25 years, working across large-scale infrastructure, DevOps, and site reliability engineering. I became an early Kubernetes end user back in 2013, before the 1.0 release, and I've been passionate ever since about helping others adopt and succeed with cloud native technologies.I currently serve as co-chair of the CNCF Technical Advisory Board and as a KubeCon + CloudNativeCon co-chair for Atlanta 2025. Before that, I was part of the Kubernetes SIG Release team. These days, my focus is on AI and machine learning workloads and building agentic experiences both on our platform and for Adobe's users. You'll often find me at CNCF and AI/ML community events in the San Francisco Bay Area, where I love connecting with practitioners, sharing what I've learned, and supporting others in their cloud native journey.

name: Peter Winter
bio: I'm a passionate technologist, always knee-deep in large-scale HPC systems. At my company, Lime Labs, I'm building and running the enterprise HPC orchestration middleware ALPCRUNCH, which uses Kubernetes as its core platform.

name: Joshua Leuenberger
bio: I have a background in server virtualization and always used Kubernetes as a control plane for my automation. Moving into an architecture position in Observability I started to specialize in Prometheus. Not much after I started to write several Kubernetes Operatots and developing our platform based fully on Kubernetes. Now I am Business Lead Kubernetes and work tightly with Capsule, Cilium, Kyverno and Crossplane on a daily basis. My main business besides owning the k8s platform is onboarding of new teams into our custom cloud using Crossplane as our daily driver.

name: Juan Camilo Restrepo Veintemilla
bio: Senior Technical Support Engineer in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and cloud‑native platforms. With over 15 years of experience supporting enterprise workloads. Focused on operational excellence, and translating complex customer cases into measurable impact through mentoring and reusable best practices. Passionate about sharing knowledge through internal documentation, workshops, and community collaboration.

name: Remi Frenay
bio: My first love was a Commodore 64. My second love is my wife, with whom I live in the center of Rotterdam — together with our two cats who occasionally try to take over my keyboard.  As a kid, I dreamed of becoming either an astronaut or a software developer. Somehow, I ended up somewhere in between: a Kubestronaut — which feels like the perfect evolution of both worlds.  I’m driven by curiosity and a deep passion for technology. I enjoy working across the boundaries of infrastructure and software, connecting systems and solving complex technical challenges. The more intricate the problem, the more motivated I become.  Earlier in my career I stepped into a management role, leading an amazing team and learning a great deal about collaboration and leadership. Over time, I realized that my strongest energy comes from hands-on engineering — designing systems, building solutions, and diving deep into technology. Today I’m back in a software development role within the cloud-native ecosystem, where I feel completely at home.  When I’m not behind a terminal, you’ll probably find me exploring new tech, learning something Kubernetes-related, or negotiating keyboard space with my cats.

name: Stéphane Noutsa
bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer specializing in building secure, scalable, and automated cloud-native platforms across AWS and Azure. My expertise spans core CNCF technologies, including Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana, Istio, ArgoCD, with strong competencies in cluster security, GitOps, and end-to-end observability. I design and operate high-reliability infrastructure, blending cloud best practices with modern DevOps methodologies.

name: Thor Wei Jie
bio: I'm Wei Jie, an AI Developer at SAP. I specialise in Kubernetes, Istio, Knative, Kserve and Argo. I’m passionate about helping developers understand and adopt modern infrastructure tools to make their architecture more scalable and performant.

name: Jorin Vermeulen
bio: I'm an IT-enthusiast with a deep passion for Webdevelopment, Cloud Engineering and Kubernetes. From an early age I've explored the intricacies of computers, building skills in programming, PC building, and server management. Over the years I've managed various servers and developed numerous applications and websites. Currently, my professional focus is at DevOps and Cloud Engineering, with the goal of becoming a Kubernetes specialist.

name: Kanav Peer
bio: I'm Kanav Peer, a DevOps and Cloud Infrastructure Architect with over 11 years of experience in the IT industry, primarily in the enterprise financial domain. My core expertise includes Kubernetes, AWS, Linux, Terraform, CI/CD, Python, with a strong focus on automation, scalability, and cloud-native design. I'm passionate about simplifying infrastructure through the “everything as code” approach. Currently, I'm exploring how Generative AI can enhance cloud infrastructure and DevOps workflows.

name: Francisco Ortiz Sarria
bio: I currently work as a platform engineer providing support for the internal platform for developers. My knowledge covers many areas, such as security, architecture, solutions, approaches and new technologies, always focused on the cloud and open source technologies. I am passionate about technology, especially everything related to Kubernetes and Linux.

name: Heyko Oelrichs
bio: I am a Principal Software Engineer at Microsoft with over a decade of experience in cloud-native development and Azure solutions. I’m passionate about Kubernetes and containerized workloads, and I’ve led the design and delivery of large-scale, enterprise-grade platforms for critical customers worldwide. Beyond engineering, I actively share knowledge and contribute to building resilient, scalable systems that empower teams to innovate with confidence.

name: Kranthi Kumar Bitra
bio: I am a technology leader driving AI-First Software Engineering and Technology Transformation initiatives for global enterprises. With deep expertise in Kubernetes, cloud-native modernization, and distributed systems architecture, I help organizations adopt modern platform engineering practices at scale. I write about cloud native technologies, AI transformation, and DevOps practices on my blog: https://kranthib.github.io/tech-pulse/

name: Win Kay Kay Khine
bio: I lead the end-to-end SDLC, designing and delivering Kubernetes-based, cloud-native platforms using CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, and Argo CD. I work closely with architects to build production-ready, containerized and multi-tenant systems, integrating IoT monitoring for real-time edge visibility. I continuously improve reliability and scalability through testing, code reviews, and cloud-native best practices.

name: Muhammad Mohsin Alvi
bio: Muhammad Mohsin Alvi is a DevOps and Platform Engineer with deep expertise in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies. He is a Kubestronaut, having earned all five Kubernetes certifications, with hands-on experience in Kubernetes security, service mesh (Istio), GitOps, and cloud-native networking. Mohsin works extensively with production Kubernetes environments, focusing on scalability, security hardening, and operational excellence. He is passionate about continuous learning, certification-driven mastery, and applying cloud-native best practices in real-world enterprise platforms.

name: Ahmed Elmelegy
bio: DevOps Engineer with expertise in cloud-native technologies and a strong focus on infrastructure as code. I design and operate resilient containerized environments, prioritizing high availability and cost efficiency. Committed to continuous learning, I constantly explore new methods to streamline CI/CD workflows and enhance system observability.

name: Anurag Kumar
bio: I'm contributor and maintainer of KubeArmor (A CNCF runtime security project). I'm interested in Kubernetes Security. You can read my blogs here https://kranurag.devWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be a CNCF ambassador because I'll try to bring and educate more people towards CNCF ecosystem. CNCF is one of the most welcoming community. I also want to share my knowledge when it comes to Kubernetes and other CNCF projects to new peoples who're getting started. Finally, I would love to meet and connect with fellow CNCF ambassadors.

name: Bartłomiej Zapotoczny
bio: I'm Bartłomiej Zapotoczny, a DevOps engineer and cloud-native enthusiast. I’m passionate about Kubernetes, automation, and modern infrastructure. Beyond the technical side, I enjoy engaging with the DevOps and Kubernetes community, sharing knowledge, and continuously learning from others.

name: Andre Ciou
bio: Andre Ciou who is an experienced infrastructure engineering and now is workings like a leader into infrastructure team in financial banking company. Few years experiences in enterprise kubernetes for technical support, pre-sales support..etc. Finally, it's so great to completed the exams, join a kubestronaut program and also that is not the ended for me, it have to be keeping in "learning launched" like a kubestronaut. And now say something for myself and "well done".

name: Dario Duff
bio: I’m a DevOps Engineer with several years of hands-on experience in Kubernetes and cloud-native infrastructure. I specialize in running production Kubernetes clusters, implementing robust deployment patterns, and building automation that fully leverages Kubernetes capabilities. I’m passionate about GitOps practices and enabling full automation across cloud environments to eliminate manual operations. My goal is to advance Kubernetes best practices and help organizations build reliable, secure, and scalable systems.

name: Floris Feddema
bio: Platform Engineer with much interest in Kubernetes. Working with Kubernetes is m passion, within my work environment and my personal homelab.

name: Olaf Schönwald
bio: I’m a DevOps engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. I specialize in tools like ArgoCD and Helm, enabling teams to build scalable and reliable systems. On my blog, https://blog.schoenwald.aero, I share insights and experiences to inspire innovation in the DevOps community.

name: Guillermo Vigil Rodríguez
bio: Guille is a Platform Engineer focusing his professional path in cloud, infrastructure as code, Kubernetes and DevOps methodologies. Guille is constantly learning new technologies and trying to be up to date with latest news in the industry.  Also Guille is certified in Kubernetes and a CNCF Kubestronaut; he is also certified in Terraform and in AWS with Practitioner, Solutions Architect Associate, SysOps Administrator Associate and Developer Associate.  He enjoys his personal life drinking beers with friends, playing football in a team and listening electro music.

name: Stathis Kapaniaris
bio: My name is Stathis Kapaniaris and I am a Software and Cloud Engineer, originally coming from Greece and based in Vienna, Austria. My everyday working life is around Kubernetes eco-system and various other CNCF-projects. I visit frequently CNCF hosted events, such as Kubecon and KCD. Aside from professional usage, I also use Kubernetes in private, home made projects and publish tutorials about them in https://devs-in-the-cloud.medium.com/

name: Cezary Stanisławski
bio: DevOps Engineer since 2022, I'm passionate about building infrastructure that doesn't page me at 3 AM. I enjoy the challenge of taming large-scale systems and seek to improve infrastructure resilience and operational efficiency, one YAML file at a time.

name: Anatolii Pedchenko
bio: I’m a DevOps engineer with strong hands-on experience in Kubernetes, AWS, Terraform, and CI/CD, managing large-scale EKS environments with dozens of clusters and Helm-deployed applications. I focus on cloud infrastructure automation, security, and scalability. In addition, I actively mentor engineers on Kubernetes fundamentals and high-availability design. I’m passionate about GitOps, platform engineering, and improving developer productivity through reliable cloud-native architectures. I’m applying to Kubestronauts to deepen my expertise and contribute to the Kubernetes community at scale.
location: Ivanivka Stavyshchens'kyi district, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine

name: Ilya Fedotov
bio: I'm a DevOps and Cloud Engineer with more than 15 years of experience in IT.

name: Thiha Min Thant
bio: Thiha Min Thant is a Senior Site Reliability Engineer specializing in Kubernetes, cloud-native technologies, and observability. Actively contributing to CNCF projects like LitmusChaos, he focuses on enhancing system reliability and advancing chaos engineering practices. Passionate about fostering innovation, Thiha is committed to empowering teams to build scalable and resilient infrastructure.

name: Jini Park
bio: Hi, I'm Jini Park. As a DevOps Engineer, I'm deeply interested in the cloud-native ecosystem, especially projects like Kubernetes, Istio, Prometheus, and OpenTelemetry. I'm also a contributor to the k8s localization project. I'm passionate about these technologies and happy to connect with others who share these interests.

name: Linn Pe Htut

I blog at https://www.giantswarm.io/blog/author/puja-abbassi and sometimes on the CNCF or other community blogs.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a CNCF ambassador, I'm passionate about bringing cloud native technologies to more developers and their organisations around the globe. Especially, as the community is growing both from a project perspective as well as on the end user side, I'm excited to bring the two closer together and see how we can enable end user organisations to build cloud native platforms using CNCF projects.Sessionize Profile: https://sessionize.com/puja

'k8n') is a GNU/Linux SysAdmin who's in love with the terminal.He works with curiosity and passion on GNU/Linux, Free & Open Source software, and Cloud Native technologies such as Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, Argo projects. name: Vishnu Vardhan Duvuri
bio: I’m Vishnu, once a middleware architect, now a cloud‑native explorer committed to building systems that scale with confidence. My journey began in enterprise integration, where I learned how complex systems talk to each other. Today, I channel that foundation into Kubernetes, cloud, and identity‑driven architectures that make the cloud not just powerful but practical.I live where cloud, containers, clusters, and cost optimization collide. By day I design and deploy resilient solutions that balance speed, security, and cost, cutting through complexity, automating the repetitive, and building infrastructure that runs with intent.I don’t just deploy workloads, I translate them. Whether it’s a workflow that saves hours or a policy that finally makes sense, I turn cloud chaos into clarity. With DevSecOps practices at the core of my work, I embed security into pipelines and architectures so teams can innovate quickly without sacrificing trust.  When I’m not deep in YAML, clusters, or threat models, I’m learning the next skill that keeps me relevant in an ever‑changing cloud‑native ecosystem.

name: Jonathan Bryce
bio: Jonathan Bryce is the Executive Director of Cloud & Infrastructure at the Linux Foundation, where he leads both the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) and the OpenInfra Foundation—two of the largest and most influential open source communities in the world. With over 15 years of open source leadership, Jonathan co-founded the OpenStack project, guiding its evolution into a global ecosystem that supports a multi-billion-dollar market and serves as the de facto standard for open cloud infrastructure. Before his open source career, Jonathan spent over a decade in data center infrastructure and co-founded one of the industry's earliest cloud computing companies, which grew into a billion-dollar business following its acquisition by Rackspace. Passionate about open collaboration, infrastructure innovation, and empowering users, contributors, and ecosystems worldwide, Jonathan frequently speaks at international conferences on open source strategy, infrastructure modernization, cloud-native technologies, and open source AI initiatives.
