name: Cathy Zhang
bio:
Cathy is a Principal Engineer at Confluent. She has over 20 years of software design
and development experience in a wide range of areas such as serverless, large scale distributed
VM/container orchestration and scheduling, edge computing, container runtime, service mesh,
software defined network, and high availability etc.
Cathy has been an active contributor in the cloud-native computing ecosystems including
Kubernetes, OpenStack, OPNFV, and has led the development of several open-source projects
under CNCF, Linux Foundation Networking, Linux Foundation Edge and Open Infrastructure.
She has made strong contributions to the technical direction and design, driving working group
discussion, and resolving technical disagreements. She co-authored the CNCF serverless
whitepaper, the CloudEvents specification, and the OpenStack service function chaining design.
She has served as the program committee member/track chair for several Kubecons and
OpenStack summits. She has delivered talks at various open-source conferences (Kubecon,
OpenStack Summit, Open Source Summit, OPNFV).

name: Rafael Brito
bio: Rafael "Rafa" Brito has over 30 years of industry experience in distributed computing, Kubernetes, Cloud, grid/HPC, and high-frequency trading infrastructure (HFT). Before joining StormForge, Rafa worked at VMware, Citigroup, and NYSE. Rafa co-organizes the KCD Texas and CNCF Austin Community Group, and he serves as co-chair of CNCF TAG Operational Resilience (as of 2025-2026). Rafa is the co-author of the best-selling book "Acing the CKA Exam" 2nd Edition. When not working with Kubernetes, you'll find Rafa training for marathons at the Lady Bird Trail in Austin, TX, and fanatically rooting for his Brazilian soccer team, Flamengo.

name: Adam Rogers

name: Mohammed Roshdy
bio: A Software Engineer with experience and a strong interest in backend development and DevOps, who loves learning and exploring new technologies.

name: Stéphane Este-Gracias
bio: For many years now, I have been a strong advocate for the development and promotion of free and open-source software. My passion for this cause has led me to actively participate in various initiatives, such as organizing the Cloud Native Luxembourg meetups and being part of the French localization team of CNCF Glossary. I'm dedicated to promoting the benefits of open-source software, believing it to be more secure, reliable, and conducive to innovation. Additionally, I leverage my expertise to help teams harness cloud-native technologies, fostering a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Drawing from my passion for open-source, I will strive to promote the ethos of collaboration, emphasizing the merits of open-source projects within the CNCF landscape and encouraging more entities to contribute and participate. I am committed to guiding the next generation of cloud native enthusiasts. Through mentorship programs and hands-on training sessions, I aim to reduce the learning curve for newcomers and ensure they have the necessary tools and knowledge to thrive within the ecosystem. In essence, as a CNCF Ambassador, my overarching goal is to be a catalyst for growth, learning, and innovation within the Cloud Native community, ensuring that it remains at the forefront of technological progress while upholding the values of collaboration, inclusivity, and open-source spirit.

My pivotal moment came when I was introduced to the world of containers through a course titled as "Red Hat OpenShift Administration Course" in 2017. This ignited my curiosity and I began delving into the containers and container orchestrations. Today as a Learning and Development Instructor with Red Hat India Private Limited, I focus on delivering training modules related to Red Hat OpenShift, Red Hat Ansible Automation and emerging technologies.

name: Raja Ravindra Kathi

Working in startups, I finally learnt to meditate in chaos. They give me the adrenaline that I will ever need. Thanks for stopping by, keep hustling!

name: Wojtek Świeboda

My expertise extends to managing Kubernetes clusters (EKS, AKS, GKE, and more), automating deployment pipelines, and ensuring high availability and disaster recovery strategies.

Co-organizer of KCD Netherlands and CNGC Netherlands meetups, helping out different KCDs and founding organizer of Cloud Native Days. @mbianchidev on (almost) every social media.

I’m passionate about bridging the gap between development and operations by promoting automation, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

name: Magdalena Jabłońska

Previously, he spent more than 10 years at Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense, where he implemented Agile methodologies, led multiple Scrum teams, and managed national-scale software systems. Currently at Appier, he specializes in end-to-end system reliability, exploratory testing, and cloud-native CI/CD pipelines for large-scale digital advertising systems. Kochi actively shares his technical insights and book recommendations through his Medium and Facebook blogs, where he writes about Agile, engineering leadership, and new trends in software development.

name: James Spurin
bio: With over 25 years of experience, James Spurin is an industry veteran skilled in various technical fields and adept at leading Engineering teams in top-tier Financial Services organisations. He now focuses on creating online courses and Technical Educational Content in areas such as DevOps, Kubernetes, and Cloud Native, incorporating hands-on learning experiences through container-based lab environments and has reached over 100,000 students globally. As a CNCF Ambassador, James actively advocates for Container and Cloud Native knowledge, education, and Kubernetes-based orchestration. His company, DiveInto, champions Cloud Native and consistently shares fresh content on its YouTube channel: https://youtube.com/DiveInto James has also established an inclusive community dedicated to Cloud Native learning, which can be accessed at https://community.diveinto.comWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to be a CNCF Ambassador as it aligns perfectly with my passion for creating technical educational content on Cloud Native/Kubernetes whilst also Giving Back, a core-principle of the CNCF. The role supports me in more actively engaging with a diverse global community, enabling me to learn from others whilst also sharing my own knowledge and expertise. As an Ambassador, I will contribute to thought leadership in the cloud native space and hope to provide an instrumental role in fostering a culture of knowledge sharing, learning and innovation. Throughout this journey, I am also eager to support and encourage others in pursuing CNCF Cloud Native certifications - an endeavour I am both passionate about and an advocate for. Overall, being a CNCF Ambassador is a highly rewarding and fulfilling experience that allows me to give back to the community while staying updated on the latest developments in the field.

name: Girish Cheedala

Currently, this platform is responsible to manage all the cloud-native infrastructure for all countries and brands of Natura Group. The Amazonia platform has transformed teams' experience managing their product infrastructure, improved collaboration and efficiency, reduced cognitive load, and shortened the Natura Engineering Team's overall time to market. As a result of this transformation in our operations, we won the DevOps Dozen 2022 award in the Best DevOps Transformation (Non-Vendor) category. As a GitOps enthusiast, I also implemented a fully automated Git-based operational model with ArgoCD across our 40+ Kubernetes clusters. This brought us traceability, reduced error occurrence, and enabled us to have a functioning DR environment, thus increasing the operational excellence of our cloud engineering team. When I learned about kubernetes I was facinated with all the intracacies of all the cloud native concepts part of that tech, which really highlighted how to run reliable business applications properly on a platform, as we tried to do in the .NET space, but wasn't always easy to apply resiliency, reliability concepts.  Personally, I enjoy hacking again on my dotfiles, learning about new CLIs and TUIs, and automating my house using Home Assistent. But that's only when I have some downtime from my family of 2 kids, and a dog ;)

Ben is an author, a frequent speaker at conferences, and an avid open source advocate. Among his published books are the Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) Study Guide, the Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) Study Guide, and the Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) Study Guide.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to teach beginners the basics of Kubernetes in a practical and relatable fashion. One way to learn Kubernetes effectively is by following the curriculum of the official Kubernetes certification exams. I will continue to produce hands-on learning resources in the form of written, and interactive media.

My passion lies in simplifying complex systems, improving platform reliability, and creating strong engineering foundations through mentoring and architectural guidance.

name: Shailendra Singh Chauhan

He has contributed to both the Business Value Subcommittee and Financial Services User Group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a longtime member of the cloud native community, I’m excited to evangelise its mission. I look forward to mentoring and training members of the community, no matter the technical skill set, to better understand why cloud native serves digital innovation and meets business goals. I strongly believe that every person in the community can help drive cloud native forward and am excited to help achieve this goal.

By being part of this program, I hope to make a broader impact and bring even more value to Linux Foundation and CNCF. Additionally, I am eager to learn more about the needs of users and help them understand how they can effectively use cloud native and container technology to solve their problems.

Prior to working on Kubernetes, I had extensive experience working on back-end development in C# .Net and MEAN stack and had experience developing and deploying micro-services to GCP/AWS

name: Lukasz Gryglicki

Prior to joining the CNCF, Lukasz worked remotely for companies based in the US, including Cleverstep, Jamis and Spreemo Health.

He loves to travel in polar areas, such as polar Norway, Finland and Russia. From 2011 to 2012, he was a scientist for a Polish polar expedition to the Hornsund fjord on the island of Spitsbergen, in northern Norway. Lukasz graduated from Warsaw University of Technology with a Master of Science in Engineering. He lives in a small town in Poland with his wife and two kids.

You can find more of my work and writings on LinkedIn and my technical blog.

name: Daniel D M
bio: Meet a Cloud Architect who writes code with the enthusiasm of a kid in a candy store. Armed with coffee and an insatiable appetite for automation, I transform mundane tasks into elegant solutions while Kubernetes keeps me humble with its delightful complexities.  As a devoted servant of Jesus Christ, I approach technology with the same dedication as my faith. When not wrestling with cloud infrastructure, you'll find me exploring new technologies and tools, always curious to learn what's next in the tech world. I embrace a minimalist lifestyle, finding joy in simplicity both in code and in life.  My love affair with Kubernetes stems from its beautiful chaos - where community-driven solutions tackle real-world problems head-on. Like a digital shepherd, I guide applications through the cloud, learning and growing alongside an amazing community of problem solvers.  Think of me as your friendly neighbourhood automation enthusiast, fueled by coffee and powered by faith, always ready to transform complex challenges into elegant solutions. Because in the end, technology is not just about code - it's about building bridges and growing together.  Life's journey isn't just about conquering technical challenges; it's about leaving footprints of kindness and knowledge for others to follow. After all, the greatest legacy we can leave behind is not in the systems we build, but in the lives we touch through sharing and caring.  Personal Blog: https://bookofdaniel.in/

name: Sharique Ahmed
bio: I’m a DevOps engineer who lives in the cloud-native world, working with Kubernetes, AWS, and CNCF tools to build scalable and secure platforms. Most days you’ll find me automating something, fixing something, or breaking something just to learn from it. I enjoy experimenting with CNCF technologies and having fun while navigating the ever-expanding cloud-native galaxy.

name: Firas Ben Nacib
bio: Hi, I’m Firas, a Tunisian DevOps Engineer passionate about cloud-native technologies and the CNCF ecosystem. My focus is on building scalable, resilient platforms using Kubernetes, GitOps, and open source tooling, and day to day you’ll find me on Kubernetes, Terraform, and AWS. I’m excited to keep challenging myself, learning new things, and sharing my experience with everyone.

name: Matthias Brodbeck
bio: I'm passionate about open source and cloud-native technologies, focusing on modern container orchestration and infrastructure automation. My current interests include building secure, resilient, and scalable systems, while actively exploring best practices in DevOps and GitOps. I enjoy contributing to and learning from the CNCF community and regularly experiment with new approaches.

name: Anil Yadav
bio: Anil is enthusiast about Open Sources technology and believe in technology for everyone. Working with kubernetes for last 6 years.

name: Edgaras Apsega
bio: I am a passionate technologist with a focus on cloud-native technologies, particularly Kubernetes. As a co-organizer of Cloud Native Lithuania Meetup, I have helped to build a strong community of like-minded professionals where everyone is sharing their own experience. In addition to my meetup involvement, I am a speaker at technology conferences and currently work as an SRE at Vinted, where I bring my expertise in Kubernetes to help ensure the reliability and scalability of our systems.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because it enables me to connect with like-minded other ambassadors, share our knowledges and expertise and will help to educate and promote cloud-native technologies to a wider audience. The role also provides access to exclusive events and resources, as well as opportunities for professional growth and career development. Overall, being a Cloud Native Foundation Ambassador is a great way to give back to the community!

name: Mohamed Shibli Alfathih
bio: Senior DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer with 10+ years of experience designing and operating highly available cloud platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
location: Al Warqaa 1, Dubai, United Arab Emirates

name: Dimitrios Vasilopoulos
bio: Passionate Cloud & Container Security Architect, focused on securing cloud-native ecosystems, driving secure DevOps, and empowering businesses to innovate safely and confidently in the cloud — at scale.

name: Håkon Solbjørg
bio: Håkon has a background in software development and an interest for DevOps. Automation, CI/CD, reproducible builds and pipelines - anything to make the builds and deploys of applications to go smooth, and when they don't, there is a reproducible build process to debug locally. It all started on linux servers before ascending to the clouds. As a software developer at heart, Håkon sees the deployment pipeline from code to running in production.
